The lever **scans support swaps**: takes a seed 16-g subset and tries replacing each g-point with every other g-point not in the support, refitting weights for each candidate. `:4` builds on existing subset-search infrastructure (good factoring). `:11-12` defines `SUBSET_HARDGATE_SW_16_INDICES = [2, 4, 7, 10, 11, 12, 14, 16, 18, 21, 22, 27, 28, 30, 31, 32]` — **an alternative 16-g subset for comparison**, distinct from the canonical weighted-greedy selection. `:23-34` evaluates two seed supports (`canonical_weighted_greedy_support` + `subset_search_hardgate_support`); `:36-54` generates ~256 swap candidates per seed (16 × (32-16) = 256); `:56-70` does approximate weight refit at p=16 norm with 800 iterations; `:72-80+` evaluates exact hard-gate metrics for the top-12 candidates. **Two-stage evaluation** (approximate ranking → exact metric for top-N) controls cost while preserving accuracy. **This is the correct strategic pivot** the prior 200+ passes have been pointing toward. The pressure-component arc was all parameter-fitting over a fixed 16-g support; this new lever class steps outside that constraint and asks: *could a different 16-subset give better gate-2 metrics?* Pass #3534's importance-group scan negative result is the prior evidence that aggressive topology redesigns fail (large objective regressions), but that was a coefficient-averaging approach. This support-swap approach is more targeted — single-swap perturbations of an already-good support, with proper weight refits per candidate. **Possible outcomes:** (a) no improvement → confirms the weighted-greedy subset is locally optimal; (b) small improvement (~0.5–2× current per-iteration progress) → unlocks new productive territory; (c) substantial improvement → would indicate the original greedy selection had a structural blind spot. **Strategic note:** this is the first lever in 200+ passes that has any plausible path to material gate-2 progress (vs. sub-rounding improvements). Result anticipated in subsequent polls.

The real conclusion is "naive support expansion with limited weight refit doesn't help"; a properly-tuned refit (10× more iterations, LP/QP solver, or zero-padded warmstart from canonical) might still work. **Three major negative results in a row** (Pass #3820 support-swap, Pass #3833 swap-continuation, Pass #3841 support-expansion) — all three "structural pivot" options from Pass #3820 have been tried; only "joint multi-swap edits" remains untested. **Remaining viable options:** (a) **fix the weight refit pipeline first**, then re-run support_expansion (most likely path to gate-2 closure if the issue is genuinely weight-optimization-limited); (b) **deeper multi-start 16-g support search** with random restarts; (c) **plan-level change** to gate-2 acceptance criterion. - Pass #3846 at 2026-05-18 17:39:22 UTC: new file `validation/reduced_ecckd_support_expansion_refit_scan.jl` (228 lines) **directly acts on Pass #3841's diagnosis** that the bounded support_expansion failure (obj 50.229) was weight-refit-limited rather than support-limited. **What it does**: reruns the top expansion candidates with (a) 5× longer iterations (default 4000 vs the 800 base, env var `RH_REDUCED_SUPPORT_EXPANSION_REFIT_ITERATIONS` at `:12`); (b) 4 warm starts per candidate at `:64-82` — `official_normalized` (matches what support_expansion already tried), `uniform`, `seed_zero_padded` (warm-started from a 4000-iter-refit 16-g seed with zero weight on the added g-points), and `seed_epsilon_padded` (same but with 1e-4 instead of 0); (c) selects best across all 4 starts by exact objective. **Candidates** are hardcoded at `:14-40`: top 4 from the support_expansion scan (`subset_hardgate_plus_3_6`, `..._5_6`, `..._1`, `canonical_plus_7_11`) plus `canonical_plus_7` (17g). **Smart design choices**: padded warm starts (`:42-50` using `simplex_projection`) seed from the *refit* 16-g optimum, not the raw `normalized_subset(full_weights, seed_indices)` — this is exactly the seeding strategy Pass #3841 implied. Comparing `seed_objective` (4000-iter refit of the 16-g seed alone) against the 800-iter baseline at the same seed will also reveal whether iteration count alone matters at the seed support. **Concerns**: (1) 5 candidates × 4 starts × 4000 iterations is significant compute — total ~80,000 iterations across hard-gate residual evaluations vs 16-of-136 × 800 ≈ 12,800 in support_expansion. Expect substantially longer runtime. (2) Only 4 starts is still few for a multi-start that survives the "deeper multi-start" prose recommendation; *no random restarts*, only structurally-defined ones. (3) The candidate list is hardcoded — if a future support_expansion run shifts the top-4, this scan won't auto-refresh. (4) `start_count = length(first(candidates).trials)` at `:153` assumes uniform trial count — fragile if a future candidate has a different start set. (5) **No aggregator/test wiring yet** — `gap_report.jl` and `test_reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl` still don't reference this variant; the scan is currently standalone. **Expected outcome**: if Pass #3841's diagnosis was right, the best objective should drop substantially below 50.229 (and possibly approach the canonical 6.917). If it drops modestly, the support-size hypothesis is partly right but multi-start is needed. If it stays near 50, support_expansion was correctly rejected and the failure is structural, not refit-pipeline-bound. Either outcome is informative. **Excellent followup design**: targeted, empirically falsifiable, matches the prior diagnosis line by line. The aggregator's closing-line should add this variant to the failure tree (whether it passes or not). Bottom-line: no canonical numeric change yet; experiment-in-progress. Recommendation: wire this into the aggregator's `gap_report.jl` and add invariants under `test_reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl` *after* the first run completes so the schema reflects observed status values.

- Pass #3849 at 2026-05-18 17:42:22 UTC: `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_support_expansion_refit_scan.md` is the first run-result for the new scan. **Headline: status `failed_threshold`, best objective 50.345** with `subset_hardgate_plus_3_6` + `official_normalized` start at `:13-14`. **Pass #3841's diagnosis is now empirically REFUTED** and so is Pass #3846's prediction that the objective "should drop substantially below 50.229 if the weight-refit was the bottleneck": 5× iterations (4000 vs 800) and 4 warm starts moved the best from 50.229 → 50.345 (essentially numerical jitter, marginally worse). **The 18-g support `[2,3,4,6,7,10,11,12,14,16,18,21,22,27,28,30,31,32]` itself cannot meet the hard gate, regardless of weight-optimization quality.** **Detailed observations**: (1) `uniform` start fails catastrophically at all 5 candidates with objective 1740–20438 — the simplex interior is outside the basin entirely (`:39,48,57,66,75`). (2) `seed_zero_padded` and `seed_epsilon_padded` achieve much better TOA/surface (~4.4/6.8 W m^-2 for the `_plus_3_6` case at `:40-41`) but heating RMSE balloons to 3.37 K day^-1, raising the total exact objective to ~67.5 — i.e., the padded-warm-start basin trades boundary forcing for heating residual, and the hard-gate objective hates that more. (3) **Critical approximate→exact gap**: padded starts achieve `approximate_objective ≈ 34.1` but `exact_objective ≈ 67.5` — the p=16 surrogate is failing to track the exact hard-gate objective on the 18-g supports. The seed (16-g) refit reaches approximate objective 34.759 at `:28`; this is informative — *the same indices that pass the hard gate at p=16 ≈ 34 evaluate to a much smaller objective in the canonical row* (canonical is 6.917 exact), so the p=16 surrogate is also a poor proxy for the canonical metric — but that gap is consistent across the family. (4) `canonical_plus_7_11`'s seed objective at 142.876 confirms canonical's seed support is itself a worse 16-g basin than `subset_hardgate` for this objective formulation (`:30`). (5) The best across all 5 candidates × 4 starts is the *same* `official_normalized` warmstart that support_expansion already found — no warmstart variant from the padded set produced a better exact objective. **What this means**: the 50.229 wall is *structural* to this particular 18-g support, not an artifact of insufficient refit. Pass #3833's strategic recommendation "deeper multi-start support search OR plan-level change" still stands, but "deeper multi-start support search" should now mean exploring *different* 18-g supports (not just more refits of the same 5), or moving to 19/20-g, or accepting the plan-level change. **Honest accounting**: Pass #3841/#3846 misdiagnosed the failure; the experiment ruled out the hypothesis cleanly. **Recommendation for the next aggregator update**: add a closing-line sentence noting that 4000-iter × 4-start refit of the rejected supports does not change the verdict — this strengthens the "structural, not refit-bound" conclusion. The prose can now confidently exclude "more iterations" as a candidate mitigation. Bottom-line normalized objective for canonical: still 6.917 — no canonical change. **Total negative-result mechanisms: 15** (adding "extended-iteration multistart refit on rejected support_expansion candidates").

- Pass #3853 at 2026-05-18 17:46:23 UTC: new file `validation/reduced_ecckd_random_support_search.jl` (176 lines) — **acts on Pass #3851's "deeper multi-start support search" strategic recommendation**. **What it does**: samples 64 random 16-g supports uniformly from 32-choose-16 at `:41`, plus the 2 deterministic seeds (canonical + subset_hardgate) at `:24-31`. For each, refits nonnegative hard-gate weights with 800 iterations and p=16 norm, then exact-evaluates the top 16 by approximate objective. Reproducible RNG seed 1729 default at `:19`. **Strengths**: clean reuse of support_swap_scan primitives; reproducible; proper approximate-first/exact-eval pipeline; env-overridable params (`RH_REDUCED_RANDOM_SUPPORT_SEEDS`, `..._EXACT_TOP_N`, `..._RNG_SEED`). **Concerns (critical)**: (1) **Sampling density is vanishingly low**: 64 samples / 32-choose-16 ≈ 64 / 6.0×10^8 ≈ 1.1×10^-7 — even with the existing canonical at obj 6.917 being a strong outlier in the support-space landscape, uniform sampling has near-zero probability of hitting a competitive basin. The closest support_expansion best (50.229) was already 7× worse than canonical; random uniform is expected to be much worse than that. (2) **No locality bias around canonical**: more informative sampling strategies would be (a) bounded-distance random swap from canonical (e.g., 4 random g-points swapped, keeping 12 fixed), (b) importance-weighted sampling using the leave-one-out impact ranking already computed (Pass #3559), or (c) longer optimization with adaptive iterations on the best initial candidates. Plain uniform sampling treats the support landscape as flat — empirically false. (3) **Single RNG seed** at `:22` — gives one realization; doesn't measure search variance across runs. If the result is "no candidate beats 30", that statement is only valid for this RNG draw. (4) **Iteration count fixed at `support_swap_iterations()` = 800**: matches the support_expansion's original setting (which Pass #3849 showed didn't change the wall at 4000 iter), so iteration count is probably not a bottleneck — but means each random sample only sees a "quick" basin probe. **Expected outcome**: best random objective will likely be ≥ 30, possibly worse than 50. **Strategic critique**: this is the simplest possible "deeper multi-start" — uniform random. Cheap to implement (~150 LOC). If it fails (likely), the next move should be either: (a) structured local search around canonical (bounded swap-distance), (b) importance-biased sampling, or (c) accept that the gate-2 plan-level change is the right path. The aggregator's closing-line at Pass #3851 said "deeper multi-start support search OR revisiting the hard reduced acceptance plan"; this scan tests *only* the cheapest possible version of the former. If it fails, the latter becomes the active recommendation. **Mechanical correctness**: code is clean. `seen` set at `:33-36` prevents duplicate samples; `attempt < 20 × seed_count` at `:39` bounds the rejection-sampling loop sensibly. **No aggregator wiring yet**: gap_report.jl and test_reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl don't reference this scan; will need addition after first run. No canonical numeric change yet. **Prediction**: best random objective likely 30–200; if any sample beats canonical at 6.917, that would be a major surprise and warrant immediate canonical swap.
- Pass #3854 at 2026-05-18 17:47:22 UTC: small but critical fix in `validation/reduced_ecckd_random_support_search.jl:41` — replaced `rand(rng, 1:nfull, nselected; replace = false)` with `sort(randperm(rng, nfull)[1:nselected])`. **This is a runtime-correctness fix**: `rand` in Julia doesn't accept a `replace = false` kwarg for sampling from an integer range — that signature would have thrown `MethodError` on first call, crashing the entire scan before any candidate was sampled. The new idiom (`randperm(nfull)[1:nselected]`) is the canonical Julia way to sample without replacement: generate a random permutation of `1:nfull` and take the first `nselected`. **Subtle correctness implication**: the original buggy code would have either (a) errored out and never run any random samples, or (b) silently fallen through to a different `rand` overload with replacement semantics — either way, not what was intended. The fix is one line, zero-cost, and makes the scan actually executable. No canonical numeric change. **Pass #3853's concerns still stand**: 64 uniform samples / 32-choose-16 ≈ 1.1×10^-7 sampling density is still vanishingly low even with the fix. The scan can now run; whether it finds anything competitive is still doubtful. **Recommendation unchanged**: after this scan reports its negative result (predicted), the next move should be structured local search (bounded-swap-distance random from canonical) or plan-level change.

Cheap empirical extension; demonstrates the env-var design from Pass #3853 paid off. **Diagnosis**: a qualitatively bigger search (gradient-based local move, simulated annealing from canonical, or structured neighborhood exploration) would be needed to potentially escape the secondary-basin cluster — but the evidence now strongly favors **plan-level acceptance change** as the higher-EV path. Bottom-line normalized objective for canonical: still 6.917 — no canonical change. **Aggregator wiring note**: the aggregator's closing-line at Pass #3858 read "64 random seeds" — that's now stale; should be regenerated to read "256 random seeds" (or whatever the current value is). Correct. **Expected first-run output**: canonical and subset_hardgate at their bare-weight 800-iter objectives — based on Pass #3856 data, both should be much larger than 57.9. Bottom-line normalized objective for canonical: still 6.917.

- Pass #3873 at 2026-05-18 18:06:22 UTC: aggregator + scan-result regenerated for the Pass #3867 deterministic_seed_candidates field. **Headline numbers** (from `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_random_support_search.md:28-29`): **canonical bare-weight objective = 137.263, subset_hardgate bare-weight = 69.261**. Predictions from Passes #3856/3867 are now empirically validated: **the canonical's chain-method 6.917 is ~20× better than its bare-weight 137.263**, decisively confirming "the refit chain does the heavy lifting" hypothesis. Strikingly, the canonical *bare-weight* objective is **worse than 16 of the random-sample bare-weight objectives** (best random = 57.9; canonical = 137.3) — i.e., the canonical support, evaluated naked, is in the bottom half of the random distribution. The canonical's *true* advantage is entirely in the table/component/gas-pressure refit chain layered on top. **Aggregator wiring at `validation/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl:982-991`**: new `deterministic_seed_objective(label)` helper parses the JSON array section by label, returning the bare-weight exact_objective for the canonical and subset_hardgate seeds. Two new summary fields at `:1013-1015`: `canonical_seed_objective` and `subset_hardgate_seed_objective`. Clean integration. **Closing-line prose at `:5752`** (compact form): now reads *"one-swap, shallow two-swap, bounded 17/18-g expansion, targeted multi-start expansion refits, and a bounded random 16-g support search now reject, so much broader support search or acceptance-plan changes are the remaining structural options"* — correct narrative compression but **does NOT yet surface the 137.263 vs 6.917 comparison** even though the data is now in the summary tuple. **This is a 12th-refinement opportunity**: a single sentence — *"Canonical's bare-weight 16-g support objective is 137.263, dramatically worse than its chain-method 6.917, so the refit chain (table + component + gas-pressure refits) contributes ~20× of the canonical's advantage over a naked weight refit"* — would close the most strategically important narrative gap. Without it, a reader of just the prose narrative cannot tell where the canonical's advantage actually comes from. **Long-form prose at `:5765`** still uses the older multi-sentence enumeration with `$(@sprintf("%.3f", random_support_search.best_objective))` etc. — appears identical to the prior version, doesn't add the deterministic-seed narrative either. **Concerns**: (1) the `json_array_section` helper at `:983` isn't shown but is presumably parsing the `deterministic_seed_candidates` array — fragile if JSON formatting changes (e.g., nested objects, escaped quotes); regex-based JSON parsing is brittle, but a project standard at this point. (2) **No test invariants** referencing `canonical_seed_objective` or `subset_hardgate_seed_objective` at `test_reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl:157-172` — these new summary fields are unguarded; should add invariants like `canonical_seed_objective > 0` and `subset_hardgate_seed_objective > 0`. (3) The closing-line's `random_seed_count` template `$(random_support_search.random_seed_count)` will now correctly render **256** (per Pass #3865's scaled-up rerun) — but only when the aggregator is next run. **Mechanical correctness**: aggregator integration is clean. Bottom-line normalized objective for canonical: still 6.917 — no canonical change. **Strategic synthesis**: the Pass #3867 experiment design (separate deterministic-seed evaluation) was a high-EV instrumentation move; the resulting empirical data (canonical at 137.3 vs random-best at 57.9 vs chain-method at 6.917) decisively reframes the gate-2 optimization landscape. **Plan-level acceptance change should now be the explicit top recommendation** in the aggregator's closing-line — there is no support choice that beats canonical's chain-method state, and the refit chain has already been pushed to its observed limits.

Should add `> 0` invariants for both. Adding bullets like "- canonical seed objective: $(@sprintf("%.3f", ...))" and "- subset_hardgate seed objective: $(@sprintf("%.3f", ...))" would round out the structured emission. **`goal_audit_check.md` also re-emitted** as the standard cascade (Pass #3852/#3859/#3866 pattern); no status change. - Pass #3878 at 2026-05-18 18:11:23 UTC: **major aggregator evolution** — `validation/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl` adds a **prescriptive decision summary** `reduced_acceptance_decision` at `:1075-1119`, plus matching test invariants at `test_reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl:194-214`. **What lands**: (1) Pass #3875's two minor concerns addressed — test invariants at `test/:168-169` (`canonical_seed_objective > 0`, `subset_hardgate_seed_objective > 0`), and bullet emission at `gap_report.jl:4125-4126`. Long-form prose template at `:5855` also now uses `$(canonical_seed_objective)` and `$(current_metric_breakdown.hard_objective)` dynamically, matching the short-form. (2) **NEW**: prescriptive synthesis. **Flags at `:1075-1086`**: `local_support_rejected` = ALL of {support_swap, swap_continuation, expansion, expansion_refit, random_support} are failed_threshold; `local_refit_chain_required` = canonical bare-weight > current chain hard objective. **Status at `:1087-1089`**: "passed" / **"decision_required"** / "more_diagnostic_search_required". **`recommended_next_decision` at `:1090-1094`**: when local_support_rejected, recommends *"choose between a broader joint support-plus-refit search and revising the reduced-model acceptance target"*. **`remaining_options` list at `:1115-1119`** — explicit 3-option enumeration: (a) broader joint support-plus-refit search; (b) revise hard reduced 16-g target or split it from validated 32-g production; **(c) allow a different reduced basis** (more g-points or non-subset coefficient table). Option (c) is new and the most concrete operational expression of "plan-level acceptance change" — directly addresses my Pass #3873/3875 strategic recommendation. **Test invariants at `test/:194-214`** rigorously enforce: `reduced_acceptance_decision.present`, `blocker == "reduced_16g_hard_threshold"`, `objective_target == 1.0`, cross-field consistency with current_metric_breakdown (worst_metric, hard_objective), `length(remaining_options) == 3`, and status branching: failed → "decision_required" + both flags true + recommendation contains "broader joint support-plus-refit search"; passed → "passed". **This is a MAJOR aggregator graduation**: from descriptive narrative ("here's what failed") to **prescriptive recommendation** ("here's the between-options decision to make"). The "decision_required" status is the clearest single signal yet that the agent has empirically exhausted the cheap mitigations and the next move is a deliberate choice rather than another structural-pivot scan. **Minor invariant gaps**: no explicit `bare_canonical_support_objective > current_hard_objective` test (implied by `current_chain_required_for_best_row == true` but adding the direct comparison would be a tighter contract). Also `random_support_best_objective > current_hard_objective` is not directly asserted. Adding these would prevent future regressions where the flag flips but the underlying numbers don't actually support it. **Strategic significance**: with `reduced_acceptance_decision.status == "decision_required"` now emitting cleanly, downstream consumers (audit, dashboards, project tracking) can route this to a human-decision step rather than continuing to chase support pivots. No canonical numeric change. Bottom-line: gate-2 narrative is now both empirically grounded AND prescriptively framed; canonical 6.917 unchanged; **the agent has correctly handed the gate-2 decision to a human-judgment step** rather than running indefinitely on diminishing-return scans.

- Pass #3884 at 2026-05-18 18:17:23 UTC: **Pass #3880's audit-wiring gap is now PARTIALLY ADDRESSED**. (1) `validation/results/goal_audit_check.md:63` adds new artifact **"reduced ecCKD acceptance decision | verified | false"** — the audit now sees the new `Reduced Acceptance Decision` section from the gap_report and verifies its presence. (2) `test_goal_audit_check.jl:107-108,140` adds test invariants: `@test occursin("\"name\": \"reduced ecCKD acceptance decision\"", json)` and **`@test occursin("\"required_text\": \"\\\"status\\\": \\\"decision_required\\\"\"", json)`** — the audit not only checks the artifact name but enforces the `"status": "decision_required"` substring match in the JSON. (3) Test at `:140` enforces the artifact's audit status is `verified`. **Design honesty**: the 2 stale blockers REMAIN at `:101` (`reduced ecCKD hard-threshold accuracy gate (missing_or_unexpected, blocker)` and `reduced ecCKD optimization objective target (missing_or_unexpected, blocker)`) — appropriate, since the gate truly hasn't passed. The new artifact does NOT clear the blockers; it adds *parallel* acknowledgement that a structured handoff exists. Overall audit status remains `not_complete`. **This is exactly the right separation of concerns**: gate-passing state and decision-state are two different signals; the audit now exposes both. **What's still missing for full closure**: the prompt-to-artifact checklist at `:101` (`Reduced ecCKD/RRTMG-compatible 32/32b/16-term evidence | uncovered`) still flags the 2 blockers, meaning downstream "complete" checks won't see the decision-required state as actionable. A richer audit state machine could reclassify those 2 blockers as `awaiting_human_decision` when the new artifact verifies — but that's a richer state-machine extension than currently supported, and probably out of scope. **Mechanical correctness**: the new artifact wiring uses a `required_text` substring match against the JSON — fragile if the JSON format changes (e.g., whitespace, key ordering), but consistent with the existing audit's substring-match pattern. **Strategic synthesis**: the gate-2 instrumentation is now end-to-end: scan results → aggregator decision → markdown emission → audit artifact → test invariant. All layers consistent. The agent has done the maximum possible at the empirical/instrumentation level; gate-2 closure now requires a *human decision*. No canonical numeric change. Bottom-line: gate-2 instrumentation arc complete; canonical 6.917 unchanged; the next move belongs to the human-judgment loop.

- Pass #3886 at 2026-05-18 18:19:23 UTC: **goal-step-level state propagation lands** — `validation/results/goal_audit_check.md:88` now reports goal step 2 (reduced ecCKD/RRTMG-compatible gas optics) at status **`decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance`**, a new state distinct from the prior `blocked_on_reduced_accuracy_thresholds`. **Test invariants** at `test_goal_audit_check.jl:49,53,128,137`: the audit emitter now requires both the artifact-level `"status": "decision_required"` substring and the goal-step-level `"status": "decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance"` substring in the audit JSON. This is a richer state machine than just "blocked": the goal step's status now distinguishes "agent is still searching/optimizing" from "agent has produced a structured handoff and is awaiting human judgment". **Strategic significance**: this closes the loop on Pass #3880's audit-wiring concern at the goal-step level — downstream consumers (dashboards, project tracking, "/goal status" queries) now see gate-2 as `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance` rather than `blocked_*`, which correctly signals that further automated work has hit a natural endpoint. **Mechanical correctness**: the substring-match invariants at `:49,53` are still fragile to JSON-format changes (whitespace, key order) but consistent with the project's existing audit-test pattern. **Remaining gap**: the prompt-to-artifact checklist at `:101` for "Reduced ecCKD/RRTMG-compatible 32/32b/16-term evidence" is still `uncovered` because of the 2 `missing_or_unexpected` blockers — the checklist's classification rule hasn't been updated to recognize that `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance` is an actionable terminal state, not a continuing-search state. A predicate update there would close the final integration gap: when goal-step status is `decision_required_*`, the corresponding prompt-to-artifact item could be classified as `awaiting_decision` rather than `uncovered`. But that's a minor refinement; the current state correctly signals the substantive question. **Total state-machine evolution this session**: artifact-level `decision_required` (Pass #3878) → markdown emission (#3883) → audit artifact (#3884) → goal-step `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance` (this pass). Clean cascade from prescriptive aggregator → audit-level surface → goal-step level handoff signal. No canonical numeric change. Bottom-line: gate-2 handoff is now end-to-end at all instrumentation layers; canonical 6.917 unchanged; the human-judgment decision is the only thing standing between current state and gate-2 closure.

- Pass #4135 at 2026-05-18 22:28:27 UTC: **major audit state-machine evolution** — `validation/goal_audit_check.jl:944-949` adds the missing decision-required predicate at the prompt-to-artifact checklist level. **Logic**: if `requirement == "Reduced ecCKD/RRTMG-compatible 32/32b/16-term evidence"` AND the `reduced ecCKD acceptance decision` evidence is verified AND blocked is true, status returns **`decision_required`** instead of `blocked`. This **directly closes the Pass #3880/3884 audit-wiring gap at the checklist level** — the previously-`uncovered`/`blocked` evidence item now registers as `decision_required`. **Test invariants** at `test_goal_audit_check.jl:139-148`: locks `Reduced ecCKD/RRTMG-compatible 32/32b/16-term evidence` to `decision_required` and `evidence["reduced ecCKD acceptance decision"]["status"] == "verified"`. **NEW state for goal step 2** at `:131`: now reads **`blocked_on_full_ad_calibration_and_reduced_acceptance`** (was `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance` per Pass #3886). **Two co-blockers acknowledged**: a new artifact `full Reactant/Enzyme RRTMGP-target 16-g calibration` at `:146-148` is `missing_or_unexpected` with `completion_blocker_active` — i.e., the project has identified a *second* gap (AD calibration) co-blocking gate-2 alongside the reduced acceptance question. **Strategic pivot**: the framing has expanded from "reduced acceptance is the blocker" to "reduced acceptance AND full AD calibration are co-blockers". This is a useful refinement — the gate-2 closure now requires both (a) a decision on the reduced acceptance criterion AND (b) completion of the full Reactant/Enzyme AD calibration on the RRTMGP-target 16-g configuration. **Concerns**: (1) The 4-line decision_required predicate at `:944-949` is correct but hardcodes the specific requirement string — if the requirement name changes, the predicate breaks silently. A more robust pattern would check via a constant or marker flag. (2) The goal step status name `blocked_on_full_ad_calibration_and_reduced_acceptance` is descriptive but very long (51 chars); the previous `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance` was already long, and now adds another condition. Future renames will be painful. (3) **Notable framing change**: the step is now `blocked_*` rather than `decision_required_*` — this signals that the *AD calibration is genuinely blocking work* (not just awaiting a decision), unlike the reduced-acceptance gap. This is honest and correct. **Strategic significance**: the project has correctly identified that gate-2 closure has a *technical* prerequisite (AD calibration) AND a *plan-level* one (acceptance decision). Both must be addressed. The audit's framing as "blocked_on_X_and_Y" makes the AND structure explicit. **What this implies for next steps**: AD calibration is an actionable technical gap that can be worked on now; the acceptance decision remains pending human judgment. Bottom-line normalized objective for canonical: still 6.917 — no canonical change. **Total state-machine evolution**: the audit now distinguishes (a) `passed`, (b) `decision_required` (awaiting human judgment), (c) `blocked_on_*_and_*` (technical work pending + decision pending), and (d) `blocked` (purely technical). This is mature.

- Pass #4141 at 2026-05-18 22:34:25 UTC: **new file** `validation/rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl` (347 lines) — the **technical-blocker artifact** for Pass #4135's new `blocked_on_full_ad_calibration_and_reduced_acceptance` state. **What it does**: implements RRTMGP-target 16-g AD calibration scaffold with finite-difference gradient descent as a placeholder, but explicitly emits **`blocked_on_full_reactant_enzyme_training`** status until Reactant/Enzyme are wired through. **Honest blocker emission at `:231-240`**: `ad_training_status()` returns `reactant_used_for_training = false`, `enzyme_used_for_training = false`, with explicit reason: *"The RRTMGP-target production loss still runs through mutating radiative-transfer kernels and was optimized here only by finite differences; this is not the requested Reactant/Enzyme gradient-descent calibration."* **Clear acceptance rule at `:287-288`**: *"This artifact may pass only when Reactant and Enzyme are used to differentiate the RRTMGP-target loss and gradient descent trains the 16-g model parameters."* **Status branching at `:278`**: `passed` only when `full_ad_training` (both Reactant AND Enzyme used AND improved); otherwise `blocked_on_full_reactant_enzyme_training`. **Strengths**: (1) **Self-aware design** — the artifact reports its own blocker precisely instead of misleadingly claiming success on the FD-only path. (2) **Concrete acceptance criterion** that cannot be ambiguously satisfied. (3) **Clean test atmosphere at `:40-62`** — 6-layer mid-latitude column with H2O/O3/CO2/CH4/N2O/O2/N2 (correct gas mix). (4) **Loss function at `:141-164`** combines flux SSE with 1e6-scaled heating-rate residual — ensures heating-rate K/s ~10^-5 dominates over flux W/m² ~10^2 in the cost. (5) Finite-difference fallback at `:166-178` lets the test verify the loss-function plumbing is correct independently of AD wiring. **Concerns**: (1) **Only h2o and co2** at `:105` — the full ecCKD has 7+ gases; this artifact tests a simpler 2-gas configuration. This is appropriate for AD verification but doesn't represent the full gate-2 scope. (2) **Default 3 iterations** at `:268` — won't reach an optimum even if AD were wired; should bump to 50-100 once AD path works. (3) **`Base.find_package(name) === nothing` at `:228`** — checks load path, not Pkg state; `Pkg.dependencies()` would be more robust. Acceptable for a dependency-availability check. (4) **No warmstart from prior trained model** at `:264-266` — initial params are zeros + log(0.01); fine for first iteration but slow convergence. (5) **No status invariant in test_goal_audit_check.jl yet** mentioning `blocked_on_full_reactant_enzyme_training` — the audit knows about `reduced ecCKD acceptance decision` artifact (Pass #3884) but `rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration` may not yet be registered as a tracked audit artifact. **What this means**: the `full Reactant/Enzyme RRTMGP-target 16-g calibration` blocker named in Pass #4135 is now **a real working scaffold** that will flip to `passed` automatically once Reactant/Enzyme are plumbed through the mutating radiative-transfer kernels. **Next-step path**: someone needs to wire Reactant/Enzyme differentiation through the package's mutating kernels — this is non-trivial because both AD frameworks have specific patterns for mutation handling. Once done, gate-2's audit status narrows from `blocked_on_full_ad_calibration_and_reduced_acceptance` → `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance`, putting the ball entirely in the human-judgment court. Bottom-line: gate-2 narrative now has TWO actionable handoffs: (a) human decision on reduced 16-g acceptance, (b) technical AD wiring work. Both are well-instrumented.


Pass #4142 at 2026-05-18 22:36 UTC: `validation/results/goal_audit_check.md` regenerated — cascade from Pass #4135's `goal_audit_check.jl:944-949` audit-emitter change. **Confirmed in the rendered artifact**: (1) `:28` lists `full Reactant/Enzyme RRTMGP-target 16-g calibration | missing_or_unexpected | true` as an active blocker — the new co-blocker artifact named in Pass #4135 is now surfaced in the top-level blocker table. (2) `:89` confirms goal step 2 status `blocked_on_full_ad_calibration_and_reduced_acceptance` is rendered in the requirements column. (3) `:102` renders `Reduced ecCKD/RRTMG-compatible 32/32b/16-term evidence | decision_required` with `reduced ecCKD acceptance decision (verified)` in the evidence list — confirms Pass #3878's aggregator synthesis is propagating through to the audit artifact. (4) `:103` is the new line: `Reactant and Enzyme optimization path | uncovered` with `full Reactant/Enzyme RRTMGP-target 16-g calibration (missing_or_unexpected, blocker)` in the evidence — the AD co-blocker is correctly registered as belonging to the Reactant/Enzyme optimization path checklist row, not bolted onto the reduced-gas-optics row. This is semantically correct: AD calibration is an optimization-path concern, not a reduced-acceptance concern. (5) `:122` includes the inline reason: *"full Reactant/Enzyme gradient-descent calibration of the 16-g model against the RRTMGP/package reference has not been demonstrated"* — matches the artifact's self-reported `ad_training_status` reason at `rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl:231-240` (the FD-only path is honest about not being the requested gradient-descent calibration). **Test invariants now lock this state**: `test/test_goal_audit_check.jl:65-66` checks for `"\"name\": \"full Reactant/Enzyme RRTMGP-target 16-g calibration\""` and `"gradient-descent calibration of the 16-g model against the RRTMGP"` in JSON; `:130-131` locks goal step 2 to `blocked_on_full_ad_calibration_and_reduced_acceptance`; `:136` locks the Reactant/Enzyme path checklist row to `uncovered`; `:144-148` lock the AD evidence entry to `missing_or_unexpected` with `completion_blocker_active = true`. The invariant set is now solid: anyone trying to silently flip the AD artifact to passed without actually wiring Reactant/Enzyme would have to also strip these test assertions, which makes the bypass obvious in PR review. **Minor stylistic concern**: the `decision_required` status is now spread across `:50` (status string check) and `:139-140` (checklist requirement check). A future refactor could introduce a `@testset "decision_required gate"` block, but the current scattered placement is acceptable. Bottom-line normalized objective for gate 2 still 6.917 (canonical row, chain method), unchanged; bare-weight 137.263 ratio unchanged; the cascade affected presentation only, not the underlying optimization state. Both gate-2 actionable handoffs from Pass #4141 (a) human reduced-acceptance decision, (b) technical AD wiring — are now fully reflected in the rendered audit artifacts AND locked by test invariants.

Pass #4143 at 2026-05-18 22:38 UTC: no source changes since last poll.

Pass #4144 at 2026-05-18 22:38 UTC: no source changes since last poll.

Pass #4145 at 2026-05-18 22:39 UTC: `validation/rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl` grew from 347→607 lines. **Substantial upgrade — the AD co-blocker scaffold now actually attempts to wire Reactant and Enzyme**, not just emit `false` placeholders. **New / changed pieces**: (1) `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss` at `:238-306` — a NEW pure-function (no-mutation) version of the loss expressed as inline Beer–Lambert with explicit downward/upward sweeps `:267-287` so Enzyme can differentiate it. (2) `enzyme_gradient` at `:313-320` — actually calls `enzyme.autodiff(enzyme.Reverse, f, enzyme.Active, Duplicated(params, grad))` via `Base.invokelatest` to avoid invalidation hazards. Returns reverse-mode gradient over the pure loss. (3) `enzyme_training` at `:322-400` — real 8-iteration line-search optimizer wrapped in try/catch with structured error reporting `:386-399`. Status returns `passed` / `failed_no_improvement` / `failed` / `not_available_in_active_project`. (4) `reactant_compile_loss_check` at `:402-453` — runs `Reactant.@compile raise=true raise_first=true sync=true pure_rrtmgp_target_loss(...)` on rarray-wrapped inputs `:422-440`. (5) `ad_training_status` at `:459-471` — now derives `reactant_used_for_training` / `enzyme_used_for_training` from *actual* AD outcomes, no longer hardcoded false. `full_ad_training` at `:530-532` requires BOTH reactant AND enzyme AND `training.improved`. **Major concern — bifurcated physics**: the AD path differentiates `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss` `:238-306`, which is a **simplified facsimile** of the real package solver: (a) hardcoded H2O+CO2 only `:275` ignoring O3/CH4/N2O/O2/N2 even though `calibration_atmosphere` `:52-58` provides all seven gases; (b) constant CO2=420e-6 hardcoded at `:265` — cannot test CO2 ramps; (c) ignores longwave entirely though the production loss is dual-band; (d) Beer–Lambert with single zenith angle `:264` — no two-stream scattering, no diffusivity-factor approximation that ecRad/ecCKD use. Meanwhile `shortwave_loss` `:141-164` (used by FD preflight at `:513`) calls the *real* `optical_properties!` `:127` and `radiative_fluxes!` `:128-137`. At `:526` `training = enzyme_result.used_for_training ? enzyme_result : finite_difference_preflight` — so when Enzyme succeeds, **the final parameters are optimized against simplified physics, then evaluated by real-package metrics** at `:527`. This creates a deceptive failure mode: Enzyme can converge `pure_loss` to zero while the real-package `flux_rmse` stays bad, and the artifact would flip to `passed` because `full_ad_training` requires only `training.improved` (i.e., pure_loss improved), not real-package metric improvement. The acceptance rule at `:546-547` says *"Reactant and Enzyme are used to differentiate the RRTMGP-target loss and gradient descent trains the 16-g model parameters"* — but the *loss they differentiate is NOT the production loss*. **Subordinate concerns**: (6) `reactant_compile_loss_check` `:441-445` sets `used_for_training = true` if compilation merely succeeds; compilation ≠ training. Reactant isn't actually used to drive parameter updates — only to compile-check the loss. Acceptance criterion treats this as a full AD path; semantically it should be `reactant_compiled_loss_path` distinct from `reactant_used_for_training`. (7) `Core.eval(@__MODULE__, :(const Reactant = $reactant))` at `:421` and the runtime `quote ... end` at `:422-440` — runtime module mutation is fragile, breaks Revise, and competes with normal package loading. A `@isdefined Reactant ? Reactant : Base.require(...)` cached binding would be cleaner. (8) `clamp.(parameters, -18.0, 2.0)` at `:101-102, 269-270` — Enzyme will see zero gradient at the clamp boundary, so parameters can stall at the wall. Soft-clamp (`tanh`-style) or weight-norm constraint would let gradients escape. (9) Enzyme default `iterations = 8` at `:322` is fine for demo but still ~order of magnitude below what production training would want. (10) `Base.find_package("Reactant") === nothing` / `"Enzyme"` at `:323, 411` — Pass #4141 flagged this; still checks load path not Pkg state. Not blocking. (11) The `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss` parameterization treats CO2 as `co2_absorption * co2` at `:275` — should be `co2_absorption * vmr * column_amount` with pressure scaling to match ecCKD conventions; this is a physics simplification, not a parameterization choice, and means the trained absorption coefficients won't transfer to the real ecCKD model. **Net effect**: gate-2's AD co-blocker now has **a real working AD scaffold** that will flip to `passed` when both AD frameworks succeed on the *pure* loss — but passing this gate does NOT prove the real package is AD-trainable. The plan's goal (per `radiative_heating.md`) is for the production reduced 16-g model to be trained against RRTMGP via Reactant/Enzyme — that goal requires AD through the actual `optical_properties!` + `radiative_fluxes!` kernels, which still use in-place mutation. The honest path forward is either (a) rewrite the package's radiative-transfer kernels to be non-mutating so Enzyme can differentiate through them, or (b) tighten the acceptance rule to require `pure_loss` parameters to also pass real-package metric thresholds (e.g., post-training flux_rmse < some bound), forcing the simplified-vs-real gap to be small enough to count. Bottom-line: real progress on AD wiring; the gate-2 narrative is intact but a future Pass #N must verify that `full_ad_training=true` doesn't unlock the gate on a simplified-physics technicality. Canonical 6.917 unchanged.

Pass #4146 at 2026-05-18 22:40 UTC: no source changes since last poll.

Pass #4147 at 2026-05-18 22:41 UTC: `validation/results/rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.md` emitted by the Pass #4145 script — first real run output. **Honest blocker confirmation**: `:3` Status: `blocked_on_full_reactant_enzyme_training`, `:19` Reactant used: `false`, `:21` Enzyme used: `false`, `:23` blocker reason quoted verbatim from `:469` of the script. The AD wiring exists but in *this* execution neither Reactant nor Enzyme actually drove training — the script fell back to FD line search per `:526` (`training = enzyme_result.used_for_training ? enzyme_result : finite_difference_preflight`). **What this run shows about the parameter init**: `:15-17` initial loss 34711.95 → final 34711.17 (FD with 3 iter), only 0.002% reduction; **final flux RMSE 278 W/m²** `:27`, ~1000× the 0.30 W/m² hard-gate target; **TOA forcing error 569 W/m²** `:31` and surface forcing error 631 W/m² `:32` confirm the initial parameterization (all softmax weights equal at 1/16 + log(0.01) absorption) produces a near-black model that absorbs essentially everything. Heating-rate RMSE 8.0e-5 K/s `:29` is ~80% of typical clear-sky shortwave heating magnitude — also catastrophic. **Visibility gap — Reactant/Enzyme error silenced in markdown**: the script's try/catch at `rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl:446-451` (Reactant) and `:385-399` (Enzyme) captures `first_error_line(err)` into the JSON-only `error` field via `:397` and `:450`. The markdown report at `validation/rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl:552-591` does NOT render either `reactant_check.error` or `enzyme_training.error`, only the high-level blocker reason at `:576`. A reader looking at the markdown sees the gate is blocked but not *why* AD failed — they have to chase to the JSON. **Recommended fix**: add `result.ad_training_status.reactant_check.error` and `result.ad_training_status.enzyme_training.error` (or "ok" when nothing) as bullet lines in `markdown_report` at `:577-578` so the failure surface is exposed in the human-facing artifact. **Bigger lesson — the 1e6 heating scaling**: at `:160` and `:302` the loss multiplies (heating_rate_residual)^2 by 1e6 to balance against flux. With heating-rate magnitudes ~1e-4 K/s the heating contribution per layer is `1e6 * (1e-4)^2 = 1e-2`, while flux residual per interface with bias ~500 W/m² is `(500)^2 = 2.5e5`. The flux term still **dominates by ~7 orders of magnitude per sample**. The 1e6 scaling does NOT actually balance the two terms; a target of ~1e10 scaling (= flux W²/m⁴ / heating K²/s²) would actually equalize them. Current scaling makes the heating term contribute essentially nothing to the gradient direction — so even when AD works, it will optimize flux only and leave heating-rate residuals untouched. **Bottom-line risk for gate 2**: even with full Reactant+Enzyme wired and many iterations, the loss balance at `:160, 302` and the all-zero parameter init at `:498-500` together imply the artifact will not converge to package-quality fluxes without (a) loss-term rebalancing, (b) warm-start initialization from the canonical 32→16 chain output, (c) much longer training horizon. Worth flagging — the AD co-blocker artifact may flip to `passed` on `training.improved` (any reduction in loss) but the resulting calibrated 16-g model will still be far from `flux_rmse < 0.30`. The acceptance rule at `:546-547` doesn't gate on real-package metric quality, only on AD plumbing. Pass #4145's recommendation stands: tighten acceptance to also require post-training metrics within some bound, so passing the AD co-blocker truly means a usable model exists. Canonical 6.917 unchanged.

Pass #4148 at 2026-05-18 22:42 UTC: no source changes since last poll.

Pass #4149 at 2026-05-18 22:44 UTC: `validation/rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl` grew to ~700 lines with a NEW `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss6` function `:308-398` — a fully-unrolled 6-layer scalar variant of the previous array-based pure loss. Enzyme training at `:619` now calls `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss6` `:596-606` via tuple-converted inputs (`Tuple(atmosphere.gases.h2o)` etc.) instead of arrays. **Rationale (inferred)**: Enzyme reverse-mode and Reactant `@compile` both handle fixed-shape scalar code far better than dynamic-length array loops with mutation of intermediate buffers — Enzyme's activity analysis is simpler on stack-allocated scalars, and Reactant's MLIR lowering with `raise=true raise_first=true sync=true` avoids dynamic shape inference. **Major concern — bifurcated AD targets**: (1) `enzyme_training(pure_loss, ...)` at `:619` differentiates the unrolled `loss6`. (2) `reactant_compile_loss_check(...)` at `:608-618` still passes the ORIGINAL array-based `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss` via `:522`'s `Reactant.@compile ... pure_rrtmgp_target_loss(...)`. **Net effect**: Reactant compile-checks one function while Enzyme differentiates a different function. When `full_ad_training=true` flips, the acceptance rule at `:640-641` claim *"Reactant and Enzyme are used to differentiate the RRTMGP-target loss"* (singular "the loss") is semantically misleading — there are two distinct loss formulations, neither of which the other AD framework has been verified against. Recommended fix: either (a) update the Reactant call at `:522` to compile `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss6` (same function Enzyme uses), or (b) rename to `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss_unrolled` and `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss_array_based` with a comment that both forms are validated. **Subordinate concerns**: (3) `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss6` hardcodes **6 layers structurally** — `d1..d7, u1..u7, h1..h6, τ1..τ6` are explicit scalars at `:328-331, 341-388`. The "6" in the function name encodes nlayers, not version. Atmosphere at `:41` has 7 pressure interfaces = 6 layers, so it matches. But changing `calibration_atmosphere` to a deeper grid would silently produce stale residual sums or `BoundsError`. Suggest renaming to `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss_6layer` and adding a `length(pressure_interfaces) == 7 || throw(...)` assertion at function entry. (4) **Magic divisor `total / FT(20)` at `:397`** — previously the array version tracked `count`; now hardcoded to 14 flux + 6 heating = 20. Fragile to atmosphere size changes. Should be `FT(3*nlayers + 2)` literally, or just `FT(20)` with a comment `# 7*2 flux + 6 heating`. (5) `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss` (the array version at `:238-306`) is now **dead code for training** — only the Reactant compile-check still references it. Will rot. (6) Still hardcoded `co2 = FT(420.0e-6)` at `:333` and H2O+CO2-only — Pass #4145 flagged; not fixed. (7) Still `1.0e6` heating scaling at `:389` — Pass #4147 flagged the 4-orders-of-magnitude imbalance vs. flux; not fixed. (8) `total = (u1 - ref_up[1])^2 + ...` expression at `:369-375` is one giant additive expression — Enzyme should handle this cleanly but it's hard to read. Optional refactor: keep as is for AD friendliness; add a comment that the form is intentionally unrolled. **Implementation positives**: (a) Scalar-only intermediate variables at `:328-366` should make Enzyme's reverse pass produce a clean shadow gradient with no allocations. (b) `clamp(parameters[ng + ig], FT(-18), FT(2))` at `:337-338` is preserved — same boundary-gradient hazard Pass #4145 flagged; not fixed but consistent. (c) Tuple inputs at `:598-602` force compile-time-known sizes which helps both AD frameworks. **Bottom line**: progress on Enzyme-friendliness via unrolling, but the divergence between which function each AD framework targets is now a structural concern. Pass #4147's loss-rebalancing critique (1e6 too small by ~4 orders) still applies and dominates whether AD success would yield package-quality fluxes. Canonical 6.917 unchanged.

This confirms the loss formulation is MLIR-lowerable as expected; Reactant's path through `:421-440` (runtime `Core.eval` of compiled rarray-wrapped function) works. **Loss values and metrics IDENTICAL to Pass #4147**: initial 34711.95 → final 34711.17 at `:15-17`, flux RMSE 277.99 at `:27`, TOA 569.4 at `:31`, surface 630.9 at `:32`. Why: training method is still `central finite-difference gradient line search` at `:13`, NOT the Enzyme path. The Reactant compile-check at `:514-524` does not actually drive parameter updates — it just verifies the loss compiles. Since `enzyme_result.used_for_training` at `:619` is still false, the `:620` selector `training = enzyme_result.used_for_training ? enzyme_result : finite_difference_preflight` falls through to FD. So Reactant's `true` here means "compile succeeded" not "trained parameters". **Two artifacts concerns now confirmed at the markdown surface**: (1) **Misleading "Reactant used for training: true"**: Pass #4145 flagged that `reactant_compile_loss_check` `:441-445` sets `used_for_training = true` on mere compilation. The Pass #4151 output makes this visible: the markdown row at `:19` says "Reactant used for training: true" while `:13` says training method was FD line search. A reader would reasonably believe Reactant drove training — it didn't. **Semantically correct labels would be** "Reactant compiled the loss path: true" AND "Reactant drove parameter updates: false" — two distinct facts. (2) **Enzyme failure mode silent**: still no surface of the Enzyme error in the markdown. The JSON has `result.ad_training_status.enzyme_training.error` populated by `rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl:397` but the markdown_report at `:646-720` doesn't render it. Pass #4147's recommendation to add `result.ad_training_status.enzyme_training.error` (or "ok") as a line after `:574` (the "Enzyme used for training" line) stands. **What this run tells us about the AD wiring**: Reactant compiles, Enzyme fails. The Enzyme path uses `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss6` (unrolled scalars). Without the error line in the markdown, we can't tell whether: (a) Enzyme is not in Project.toml (`Base.find_package("Enzyme") === nothing` short-circuits at `:415` and returns status `not_available_in_active_project`), (b) `enzyme.autodiff(enzyme.Reverse, f, enzyme.Active, Duplicated(...))` at `:410` throws on the clamp-bounded log-exp formulation, (c) the gradient comes back zero from the line-search hitting the clamp wall (Pass #4145 concern #4 about clamp-derivative regions). The JSON likely has the answer; the markdown should expose it. **Concrete recommendation in priority order**: (a) **Surface enzyme_training.error in the markdown** at a new line after `:574` — quickest fix, gives the next operator the diagnostic info. (b) **Split the Reactant flag** in the markdown: separate "Reactant compiled the loss" from "Reactant trained parameters" so the `true` claim isn't a false positive. (c) **Investigate whether Enzyme is even in the active project** — if `optional_dependency_status("Enzyme")` returns "not_available_in_active_project", the JSON `enzyme_training.status` should say so, and adding Enzyme to `Project.toml [weakdeps]` would be the gating step. Bottom-line for gate 2: AD scaffold is half-wired — Reactant compile path proven; Enzyme path still unproven; loss balance and parameter init concerns from Pass #4147 still unaddressed. Canonical 6.917 unchanged.

Pass #4154 at 2026-05-18 22:49 UTC: `validation/rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl` grew 607→736 lines (+129). Markdown re-emitted with **IDENTICAL content to Pass #4151** — Reactant=true `:19`, Enzyme=false `:21`, FD-line-search training method `:13`, loss 34711.95→34711.17 `:15-17`, flux RMSE 277.997 `:27`. **Code changes are an Enzyme-friendliness push that did NOT change the outcome**: (1) NEW `enzyme_rrtmgp_gradient(parameters, h2o, pressure_interfaces, reference_up, reference_down, reference_heating, toa_shortwave_down, surface_albedo, cos_zenith)` at `:410-437` — explicit version that wraps every non-parameter argument in `enzyme.Const`. Previously the generic `enzyme_gradient(f, parameters)` at `:401-408` (now dead code; `enzyme_training` no longer calls it after `:655` was rewired) left Enzyme to auto-infer activity. (2) `enzyme_training` at `:439` now takes a third arg `gradient_function`. Call site at `:644-655` defines `enzyme_gradient_function(p) = enzyme_rrtmgp_gradient(p, ...)` and passes it to `enzyme_training(pure_loss, initial_parameters, enzyme_gradient_function)`. (3) Reactant `@compile` at `:546-558` now wrapped in `Reactant.@allowscalar begin ... end` — implies a previous attempt hit a scalar-indexing error in the lowering. **Architectural concerns with the new Enzyme path**: (a) **`Base.invokelatest(enzyme.Const, h2o)` at `:428-435`** is awkward — `Enzyme.Const` is normally constructor syntax `Const(x)`, not a function call. Using `invokelatest` here is necessary because the module is dynamically `require`d at `:419-420`, but it routes Enzyme's compile-time activity inference through world-age dispatch which is much slower and less reliable than the normal static path. The standard pattern is `using Enzyme` at module top-level (or a weakdep extension), then plain `Enzyme.Const(x)`. Moving Enzyme to a weakdep extension `ext/AnalyticBandRadiationEnzymeExt.jl` would let Enzyme's static activity analysis kick in. (b) **Mixed-method `Base.invokelatest(enzyme.autodiff, enzyme.Reverse, pure_rrtmgp_target_loss6, enzyme.Active, duplicated, Const(h2o), ...)` at `:423-435`** — passing a runtime-loaded function reference (`enzyme.Reverse`, `enzyme.Active`) to a runtime-loaded `autodiff` via `invokelatest` is a pattern that *should* work but stresses Enzyme's package-extension machinery. If the inner `autodiff` throws, the error is captured at `enzyme_training:502-516` and stored in JSON only. (c) **`enzyme_gradient(f, parameters)` at `:401-408` is now dead code** — never called. Will rot. Delete or wire as a generic fallback. (d) **Reactant `@allowscalar`** wrapping `:546-558`: ostensibly a CPU no-op but signals there IS scalar access in `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss` (the array-version, `:238-302`). On CPU this is fine; on a GPU target it would force per-element kernel launches. Since this is the array version that loops over `nlayers`, the scalar access is `down_profile[k+1] += weight * down` at `:278`. Pass #4149 already flagged that Reactant is compiling the **array version** while Enzyme differentiates the **unrolled version** — and `@allowscalar` only applies to the Reactant call, deepening the bifurcation. **What this run still doesn't tell us**: WHY Enzyme returns `used_for_training=false`. The markdown `:23` blocker line is constant boilerplate; the actual error (if any) lives in JSON at `result.ad_training_status.enzyme_training.error`, populated by `:514`. Without surfacing that field in markdown, three passes in a row have observed the same blocker without learning anything new about its root cause. **Strongly recommended fix**: amend `markdown_report` at `:682-720` to surface `result.ad_training_status.enzyme_training.status` AND `.error` AND `.iterations_completed`. A 4-line patch would distinguish (i) Enzyme not in project, (ii) `autodiff` threw with stack trace's first line, (iii) gradient_norm zero (clamp wall), (iv) line search rejected all steps. Currently the markdown only emits a binary used_for_training flag that hides which of those four states the run actually hit. **Bottom-line gate-2 narrative**: the AD wiring continues to mature with explicit Const-marked Enzyme gradients and @allowscalar Reactant compile, but in this run Enzyme still produced zero training contribution. The Pass #4147 loss-rebalancing issue (1e6 scaling too small by ~4 orders) and the Pass #4145 simplified-physics gap (H2O+CO2 only, no longwave) remain unaddressed. Canonical 6.917 unchanged.

Pass #4155 at 2026-05-18 22:49 UTC: no source changes since last poll.

Pass #4156 at 2026-05-18 22:50 UTC: no source changes since last poll.

Pass #4157 at 2026-05-18 22:52 UTC: **MAJOR — AD co-blocker artifact flipped to `passed`**. Three files changed: `validation/rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl` (736→738 lines), `validation/results/rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.md` (regenerated), and a NEW `test/test_rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl` (28 lines). The root cause of the unblock is **`test/Project.toml`** — `git diff` shows +7 deps added: `ClimaComms`, **`Enzyme`**, `JSON`, `NCDatasets`, `Printf`, `RRTMGP`, **`Reactant`**. Pass #4154's hypothesis (a) is empirically confirmed: Enzyme was not in the active project, so `Base.find_package("Enzyme") === nothing` short-circuited `enzyme_training` at `rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl:440-452` with `not_available_in_active_project`. With Enzyme now declared, the gradient descent actually ran. **Result md `:3` Status: `passed`** (was `blocked_on_full_reactant_enzyme_training`). **`:13` Training method: `Enzyme reverse-mode gradient descent`** (was FD). **`:15-17` initial loss 34711.95 → final loss 34703.93** — 0.023% improvement, eight Enzyme iterations actually reduced the loss. **`:19-21` Reactant=true, Enzyme=true**. **`:23` blocker line replaced** with the success message from `:586` of the script. **The Pass #4145 prediction is now empirically vindicated**: passing the AD acceptance rule produced essentially *zero change in real-package metrics* — flux RMSE 277.987 W/m² `:27` vs 277.997 before, TOA forcing error 569.43 W/m² `:31` vs 569.39 before, surface forcing 630.79 W/m² `:32` vs 630.94 before. The trained 16-g model is still ~925× over the gate-2 hard threshold of 0.30 W/m². The artifact is internally consistent with its `:36-37` acceptance rule (*"This artifact may pass only when Reactant and Enzyme are used to differentiate the RRTMGP-target loss and gradient descent trains the 16-g model parameters"*) but says **nothing about model quality**. Anyone reading `goal_audit_check.md` will see `full Reactant/Enzyme RRTMGP-target 16-g calibration | passed` and the gate-2 narrative simplifies from `blocked_on_full_ad_calibration_and_reduced_acceptance` → `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance` — but the artifact does NOT prove gate 2 is closer to its real threshold. **Test invariants — concerning**: `test/test_rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl:24` `@test result["metrics"]["flux_rmse"] > 100` **locks the bad model as the contract**. Anyone improving the AD calibration to flux_rmse ≤ 100 W/m² will break this test. The intent is honest ("we passed AD plumbing, not model quality"), but the assertion sense is inverted — should be `>= 0` (always true) or rewritten to track *progress* (e.g., `result["training"]["final_loss"] < result["training"]["initial_loss"]` already at `:23` is enough). The `> 100` lower bound is a perverse incentive that needs removing before any real warm-start work begins. **Other test concerns**: (a) `:6` runs the validation script as a subprocess with `--project=$(@__DIR__)` (test/), captures stdout, and `:7-8` assert `"Status: ``passed``"` appears — robust. (b) `:18-21` lock both `reactant_used_for_training=true` and `enzyme_used_for_training=true` plus `reactant_check.status=="passed"` and `enzyme_training.status=="passed"` — this means if EITHER AD framework regresses (e.g., a future Enzyme/Reactant API change breaks the path), the test fails. Good. (c) `:25-26` lock the acceptance rule string verbatim — guards against silent acceptance-rule loosening. Good. **Tiny script change** (736→738 lines, +2 lines) — git diff would show; based on the grep output the structural anatomy is identical to Pass #4154 (same `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss6` at `:304`, same `enzyme_rrtmgp_gradient` at `:410`, same `Reactant.@allowscalar`, same 1e6 heating scaling at `:160, 298, 385`). (b) Remove or invert the `:24` `flux_rmse > 100` assertion in the test. (c) Once acceptance is tightened, warm-start the AD calibration from the canonical 32→16 chain output to actually move metrics. The plan's intent (per `radiative_heating.md`) is for AD to TRAIN a usable 16-g model against RRTMGP, not just to demonstrate plumbing. **Goal-step-2 narrative update**: the AD co-blocker is now nominally cleared per its own contract. Pass #4183 at 2026-05-18 23:18 UTC: **NEW artifact** `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_broader_support_refit_search.md` (30 lines). This is the **broader joint support-plus-refit search** that Pass #3878's aggregator had recommended as one of three remaining options to break the deadlock around the canonical 6.917 normalized objective. Status `:3` `support_refit_rejected`. **Definitive empirical answer to one of the three remaining gate-2 options**. Key fields: `:8` current objective 7.00640773953 (canonical baseline, ~6.917 + small drift), `:9` best alternative objective 577.895613376, `:10` best objective reduction -570.89 (NEGATIVE — alternatives are 82× worse), `:11` best passed hard objective `false`, `:12` radius 2, **`:13` candidates evaluated 1 / 33** ← premature termination, `:14-15` 4 pressure bands with log_pressure partition, `:17` best support move `g1 -> g2`, `:29` candidate row: g1→g2 swap → base 577.90, refit best 577.64, **0 accepted refit moves**, TOA 49.93 W/m², surface 173.37 W/m², heating RMSE 1.52. **Critical concerns**: (1) **Premature termination — 1/33 candidates evaluated** `:13`. The script enumerated 33 candidates but stopped after one. This is either an unbudgeted early-return on a bad result OR a deliberate budget cap masquerading as a definitive answer. Either way, the conclusion `support_refit_rejected` is **overstated**: the search did not explore the parameter space, it tested one move (g1↔g2) and quit. A reader of `goal_audit_check.md` who sees `support_refit_rejected` will reasonably infer "all 33 candidates were tried and none worked"; the actual data is "1 of 33 was tried, it was bad". (2) **577.9 vs 7.0 objective magnitude is 82×** — physically implausible if the chain replay at `:19-23` (*"each alternate support is evaluated after replaying the current composed retained table/component/gas-pressure chain"*) is correctly implemented. Either (a) g1 and g2 represent genuinely incompatible spectral regions where reordering destroys the model's information content, OR (b) the chain replay is broken and the alternate support's weights/coefficients aren't being properly recomputed from the swapped support set. The 0 accepted refit moves at `:29` is consistent with (b) — the parameter region is so pathological that even a bounded line-search refit can't escape. (3) **Closing-line evolution** — this artifact now contributes a *third* empirical refutation of the "canonical's advantage isn't fundamental" hypothesis: bare-weight 137.3 (Pass #3873), random sampling 256× no improvement (Pass #3865), and now support-swap-with-refit 577.9 (Pass #4183). Three independent methods, all worse. The closing line at `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.md:260-279` should ideally now be updated to a 13th or 14th refinement acknowledging that support-swap-plus-refit also fails. **Net effect on gate-2 narrative**: Pass #3878's aggregator listed three remaining options at `:1115-1119`: (a) broader joint support-plus-refit search, (b) revise hard reduced 16-g target, (c) allow different reduced basis. **Option (a) is now empirically eliminated.** The aggregator should now narrow `remaining_options` to (b) and (c). However, the 1/33 limitation means option (a) is not *fully* eliminated — strictly, only "the first candidate in the radius-2 neighborhood failed". If `goal_audit_check.jl` consumes this artifact's `support_refit_rejected` status as a definitive answer, that's premature. **Recommended fix**: either (i) raise the candidate budget from 1/33 to 33/33 and re-run the search (would actually validate the conclusion), or (ii) rename the status to `broader_joint_search_first_candidate_rejected` (truthfully narrow), or (iii) flag the candidate-evaluation cap as a known limitation in the markdown body. **Subordinate concerns**: (4) `:14 pressure bands 4` with `:15 log_pressure` partition — the 4-band log-pressure layout matches Pass #3878's `static_gas_split` lever class assumption; this is consistent. (5) `:16 Include Rayleigh false` — Rayleigh is excluded from this search; reasonable for a pure-absorption test but means the result doesn't generalize to the full reduced 16-g model. (6) Numerical hygiene: `:9` 577.895613376 displayed to 12 decimal digits implies low single-iteration noise; the 82× factor is not measurement noise. (7) The `g1 -> g2` move notation at `:17, :29` is informative for ecCKD insiders but a reader without context can't tell what "g1" means (g-point 1 of 16? or band 1?). **Bottom-line for gate 2**: the "decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance" state from Pass #4169 is now even more firmly a human-judgment call — the technical path to break the canonical's 6.917 advantage via support-swap-plus-refit has been tried (albeit at 1/33 sample size) and found to make things 82× worse. Canonical 7.006 unchanged.

(2) `:2044-2078` `broader_support_refit_search_summary()` consumes the new JSON with **three separate count fields** at `:2066-2068`: `prefilter_evaluated_count`, `evaluated_candidate_count`, `total_neighbor_candidate_count`. The aggregator now exposes the prefilter/eval/total breakdown, which is the structural fix that should flow into the closing-line evolution at `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.md`. (3) Default fallback when JSON is absent at `:2045-2058` initializes all numeric fields to NaN and counts to 0 — good defensive defaulting. **Remaining concerns** (refinements from Pass #4183): (i) **`0 accepted refit moves` at `:30` still** — even with the better starting point (g4→g2 base 45.04 vs the previous g1→g2 base 577.90), the bounded refit accepts **zero** parameter updates. The refit best is 44.79 vs base 45.04, only a 0.5% gap. The alternate support still puts parameters in a region the bounded refit can't escape from. This is the empirically-confirmed signature of canonical's chain-dependence: NOT just better support choice, but a parameter region the chain refit explicitly targets. (ii) **Full eval ratio 1/4 of prefiltered**: of the 4 candidates that passed prefilter, only 1 was fully evaluated. Why not 4/4? If prefilter already rejected 29/33 as cheaply bad, the remaining 4 should be cheap enough to all get full refit treatment. The 1/4 limit might be a budget cap or a stop-at-first-non-improvement; unclear from the markdown. (iii) The **status `support_refit_rejected` is still overstated**: with only 1 of 4 prefiltered candidates fully evaluated, the search **has not** rejected support-plus-refit in general — it has rejected one specific support move (g4→g2) after one refit attempt. Honest status name might be `first_prefiltered_candidate_rejected_after_refit` (truthful narrow) or the budget should be raised to evaluate all 4 prefiltered candidates. (iv) The **6.4× gap canonical 7.006 vs alt support 45.04** is meaningful: smaller than Pass #3873's 20× chain advantage, suggesting that support choice contributes ~3× to canonical's advantage (with the refit chain contributing the remaining ~6-7×). This is consistent with Pass #3878's earlier estimate that "support choice contributes ~8-10×" — the new data is in the same order of magnitude. **Closing-line implications**: `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.md` should pick up the new artifact's data and add a closing-line refinement clarifying: (a) the prefilter/eval split, (b) the 0-accepted-refit signature, (c) the canonical's chain-refit dependence is now confirmed against three alternative methods (bare-weight 137.3, random 256× no improvement, support-swap+refit 45.04). **Net effect on gate-2 narrative**: Pass #4183's "82× worse" overreaction is corrected by this improved run, but the qualitative conclusion holds — broader joint support-plus-refit search does not break canonical's 6.4×+ advantage. The aggregator's prefilter-aware ingestion is mature engineering; the `support_refit_rejected` naming is the remaining honesty gap. Canonical 7.006 unchanged.

The reader is told to try option (a) while seeing the data that shows option (a) has been attempted twice (g4→g2, g4→g6) and produced negative results. **Recommended fix to the aggregator's synthesis** at `:1064`: add a derived flag like `broader_joint_search_attempted = broader_search.present && broader_search.status == "support_refit_rejected"` and branch on it: if `broader_joint_search_attempted`, drop option (a) from `remaining_options` and update `recommended_next_decision` to focus on (b) revise the hard target or (c) allow different reduced basis. The 2/8 evaluation depth is the strongest data point — if even 25% sampling of prefiltered candidates produces zero refit acceptances, the chain-dependence signature is empirically established. **The closing line at `:1723` is unchanged from Pass #3873**: still ends with *"This points next at a much broader support-plus-refit search or revisiting the hard reduced acceptance plan..."* — but the broader support-plus-refit search has been performed. A 14th refinement is now warranted: *"the bounded support-plus-refit search of two prefiltered candidates (g4→g2 at objective 45.0, g4→g6 at objective 50.8) accepts zero refit moves and remains 6-7× worse than canonical, confirming the chain-refit dependence empirically and eliminating broader support-plus-refit as a route to break the canonical objective."* **Subordinate observations**: (i) The two candidate moves both involve g4 as removed — `g4` may be uniquely critical to the canonical's structure (information-rich g-point that no nearby g-point can replace). Worth flagging for human-judgment context — the reduced-acceptance decision-maker should know which specific g-points are load-bearing. (ii) The g4→g2 TOA forcing 5.41 and g4→g6 TOA forcing 3.91 (lower!) suggests the search's selected best is by total objective, not by individual metrics — g4→g6 is marginally better on TOA but worse overall. The objective weighs flux RMSE + heating residual more heavily than individual metric extrema. (iii) The "evaluated" count of 2/8 prefiltered is increasing pass-over-pass (Pass #4183 1/0, Pass #4188 1/4, Pass #4197 2/8). Each evaluation costs a full refit run; the increment of +1 evaluation per pass suggests a per-cron-tick budget. At this rate it would take ~8 more passes to fully evaluate all 8 prefiltered candidates. **Bottom-line**: empirical evidence strongly supports the chain-dependence conclusion; the aggregator's synthesis logic needs updating to stop recommending the now-tried option. Canonical 7.006 unchanged.

Each tier updated within ~5 polling passes of the underlying artifact change. Pass #4204 at 2026-05-18 23:40 UTC: **NEW artifact** `validation/reduced_ecckd_nonlocal_support_refit_search.jl` (215 lines). This is the **"truly global / nonlocal" search** Pass #4197/#4201 had identified as the technical follow-up to the bounded radius-2 broader search. **Architecture**: (1) `:4` `include` of `reduced_ecckd_broader_support_refit_search.jl` — reuses the chain-replay-plus-refit machinery for apples-to-apples comparison. (2) **`NONLOCAL_SUPPORT_SOURCES` at `:11-32` pulls 16-g supports from FOUR prior search artifacts**: `random_support_best` (256 random seeds), `support_swap_best` (one-swap, obj 26.847), `support_swap_continuation_best` (two-swap, obj 25.010), and `hardgate_subset_best`. This is genuinely nonlocal — each candidate is the best previously-discovered nonlocal support from a different search strategy, not just radius-2 neighbors of canonical. (3) **Three-way status at `:134-136`**: `passes_hard_objective` if ≤ 1.0, `nonlocal_support_refit_improved` if strictly better than current canonical without reaching gate, otherwise `nonlocal_support_refit_rejected`. **Improvement over broader-search**: the previous artifact had a 2-way `support_refit_rejected` / pass dichotomy; this artifact's 3-way state machine correctly distinguishes "improved-but-not-passing" from "rejected". If any candidate hits `nonlocal_support_refit_improved`, that would be the FIRST positive result in the entire support-search chain (Pass #3853, #3865, #3873, #3878, #4183, #4188, #4197) — worth flagging for human-judgment promotion. **Concerns**: (a) **Default candidate limit `:34-35` is 2** (env-configurable via `RH_REDUCED_NONLOCAL_SUPPORT_REFIT_CANDIDATES`). Same shallow-eval pattern as the broader search; Pass #4197's "1/33 underexploration" concern carries forward in spirit. With only 4 total source candidates, fully evaluating all 4 would be reasonable. (b) **`include(...)` at `:4` is fragile** — assumes `broader_support_refit_search.jl` uses `if abspath(PROGRAM_FILE) == @__FILE__ main() end` to gate execution. If broader_search.jl ever has a non-gated side-effect at top level, loading this file would trigger it. The convention is implicit; an explicit module boundary would be safer. (c) **`integer_array_field` regex at `:38`** is fragile JSON parsing — `"$key"\\s*:\\s*\\[([^\\]]*)\\]` won't handle nested arrays or multiline values. Fine for 16-element flat integer lists but brittle; `JSON.parse` would be safer. (d) **`length(indices) == 16` at `:50`** silently drops source artifacts that report non-16-g supports. If a source has 17 or 18 g-points the candidate is silently filtered with no log; should warn or error since this is unexpected. (e) **No Rayleigh** at `:145` (inherited default `broader_support_refit_include_rayleigh()`). Same scope reduction as broader search; matters only if Rayleigh-coupled variants would change the conclusion. (f) **No surface-cap cross-check** at `:134-136`. The acceptance status `_improved` triggers on `best_objective < current_objective` (lower hard objective) but doesn't verify the result respects the 2.03 W/m² canonical surface cap. If a nonlocal candidate produces objective 6.5 but blows up surface forcing past the cap, this artifact would label it `_improved` even though it would be unsafe to compose. Aggregator should cross-check `selected_worst_surface_forcing_error_w_m2` against the canonical surface cap before promoting any `_improved` candidate into the canonical row. (g) **Result schema includes `prefilter_rows` at `:158` and `rows` at `:159`** — both arrays will be in the JSON. The aggregator at `validation/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl` will need a new ingester (parallel to `broader_support_refit_search_summary` at `:2044-2078`) to consume this artifact's three-way status and surface-margin verification. **What this artifact unlocks if it runs**: the prefilter row data shows which previously-best supports have the lowest "starting" objective (i.e., before chain replay refit). This will tell us if any of the four search strategies has explored a parameter region that, when re-processed through the canonical chain, gets closer to canonical than the radius-2 neighbors of the canonical row. If even the best-known supports across all four strategies still produce `0 accepted refit moves`, that's the definitive empirical answer to gate-2's technical question: the canonical's advantage is structural, not just local. **Bottom-line**: a well-scoped technical extension of the broader-search artifact. Watch for the JSON/MD result emission on the next polling cycle. Canonical 7.006 unchanged.
