With the canonical chain coefficients applied to alternate supports: support_swap is 196× worse (objective 1375 vs canonical 7.006), continuation/random are **10000× worse** with heating RMSE values 3518-3965 K/day (vs canonical ~0.35 K/day — 4 orders of magnitude difference). These heating rates are physically nonsensical, indicating the canonical chain's table/component coefficients are tightly coupled to the canonical support set; applying them to a different support produces a model where the chain replay destabilizes catastrophically. The **`hardgate_subset_best` is the only viable alternate support**, and even it produces 0 accepted refit moves at objective 45.04. **0 accepted refit moves across ALL 4 candidates** = the chain refit cannot escape from ANY of these alternate-support parameter regions. (B) **`validation/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl` synthesis logic updated** — exactly the Pass #4204/#4208 recommended fix lands. Key changes: (1) `:1099-1105` NEW `nonlocal_support_refit_rejected` derived flag with predicate `present && status=="nonlocal_support_refit_rejected" && evaluated_candidate_count==total_candidate_count && best_objective>current_hard_objective` — only flips true when ALL candidates were evaluated, not just one. (2) `:1109-1117` `recommended_next_decision` is now a **4-way state machine**: `passed` / `nonlocal_support_refit_rejected` (most evidence) / `bounded_broader_support_refit_rejected` / `local_support_rejected`. When `nonlocal_support_refit_rejected` is true, the recommendation drops the "support-plus-refit search" option entirely and reads **"choose between revising the reduced-model acceptance target or allowing a different reduced basis"** — only 2 options. (3) `:1118-1130` `remaining_options` is now a **3-way branch** correspondingly: when `nonlocal_support_refit_rejected` only 2 options listed (revise target, different basis); when bounded-broader rejected 3 options including "full global/nonlocal"; when only local rejected 3 options including "broader joint". (4) NEW fields surfaced in result: `nonlocal_support_refit_rejected`, `nonlocal_support_refit_best_objective`, `nonlocal_support_refit_evaluated_candidate_count` at `:1153-1157`. **Net effect on gate-2 narrative**: gate 2 has reached the **empirical end-of-line for technical exploration**. (ii) The 3-orders-of-magnitude jump from support_swap (1375) to continuation (70366) suggests the swap continuation winner moved FURTHER from canonical than the bare swap did, and the chain's specificity to canonical penalizes departures nonlinearly. (iii) The aggregator's predicate `evaluated_candidate_count == total_candidate_count` at `:1102-1103` is the cleanest possible guard against "premature rejection" claims — only flips true when the data justifies the conclusion. Pass #4423 at 2026-05-19 03:21 UTC: **STRATEGIC DECISION RECORDED (user input, no source changes yet)**. The user has decided to resolve the gate-2 stalemate by combining audit options (b) and (c) from `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.md:278-279`: **accept the 16-g canonical model at normalized objective 7.006 as a frozen diagnostic, and shift gate-2's production target to 32-g with an added RRTMGP comparison axis**. Stated goals: (i) first match ecCKD/ecRad output at 32-g (already substantially satisfied by gate-1's cloudless ecCKD ecRad hard gate that passes at flux RMSE < 0.30 W/m²), (ii) then compare 32-g model output to RRTMGP for the same column ensemble. **Concrete edits the implementing agent should land** (none done by this review agent — read-only): (1) **`radiative_heating.md`** — rewrite gate 2 from "reduced 32→16 gas-optics gate" to "32-g production gas-optics gate with RRTMGP comparison"; document 16-g model as frozen diagnostic at canonical 7.006, not a target. (2) **`validation/goal_audit_check.jl:944-949`** — replace the `Reduced ecCKD/RRTMG-compatible 32/32b/16-term evidence` `decision_required` predicate with a new `32-g production gas-optics validated against ecCKD reference and RRTMGP comparison` requirement; transition goal step 2 status from `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance` to either `passed_32g_target` or `blocked_on_rrtmgp_comparison`; update `test/test_goal_audit_check.jl:54,130-131` in lockstep. (3) **`validation/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl:1064 reduced_acceptance_decision_summary`** — set `recommended_next_decision = "accepted: 16-g canonical (objective 7.006) locked as diagnostic; production target moves to 32-g with RRTMGP comparison"` and empty `remaining_options`. (4) **NEW artifact `validation/reduced_ecckd_32g_rrtmgp_comparison.jl`** — reuse `validation/rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl:73-87` `rrtmgp_reference_fluxes(atmosphere)` infrastructure against the gate-1 column ensemble (`ecckd_rcemip_style_column_subset.nc`, `ecckd_clear_sky_tropical_column.nc`, `ecckd_all_sky_tropical_column.nc`); emit `flux_rmse_vs_ecckd_reference` (already passing per gate 1) + `flux_rmse_vs_rrtmgp` (new) + `heating_rate_rmse_vs_rrtmgp` (new). (5) **Acceptance rule** for the new gate 2: `flux_rmse_vs_ecckd_reference < 0.30 W/m² AND flux_rmse_vs_rrtmgp < <bound>` — the first half is already passing; the second half is the new technical work. **Caveats raised**: (a) **RRTMGP is itself a CKD approximation**, not LBL truth — comparing 32-g ecCKD to RRTMGP measures *agreement between two CKD models*, not absolute accuracy. The existing gate-1 LBL comparison remains the stronger absolute-accuracy metric. The framing should be "RRTMGP-compatible production" rather than "validated against truth". (b) **16-g machinery becomes dead code** once goal shifts: the broader/nonlocal support searches (`reduced_ecckd_broader_support_refit_search.jl`, `reduced_ecckd_nonlocal_support_refit_search.jl`), the 16-g `pure_rrtmgp_target_loss6` Enzyme path in `rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl`, and the 15-refinement closing-line evolution in `reduced_ecckd_gap_report.md:1735` all lose their target. Worth a separate cleanup PR — keeping them as "diagnostic frozen state" risks confusing future maintainers. (c) **`test/test_rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl`** should be renamed/scoped to either 32-g (parallel new test) or frozen-16-g-diagnostic (keep current invariants under a documented "diagnostic" label). **Implementation order suggestion** (lowest-risk-first): (i) update `radiative_heating.md` to reflect the new gate-2 target; (ii) update `validation/goal_audit_check.jl` predicate + matching `test/test_goal_audit_check.jl` invariants; (iii) emit the new `reduced_ecckd_32g_rrtmgp_comparison.{jl,md,json}` artifact with `RRTMGPClearSkyModel` from the existing extension; (iv) update aggregator synthesis at `validation/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl:1064` + closing-line template at `:5994`; (v) defer the 16-g machinery cleanup to a separate PR. **Expected immediate state-flip after items (i)–(iii)**: goal step 2 transitions from `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance` to `blocked_on_rrtmgp_comparison` or `passed_32g_target` depending on the new acceptance rule wording, and the new `32-g RRTMGP comparison` artifact gates the final gate-2 pass. Canonical 16-g 7.006 unchanged (now permanently locked as diagnostic).

(c) `:139-167` emits BOTH `candidate_vs_ecckd_reference` AND `candidate_vs_rrtmgp` parallel metric tracks per Pass #4423's recommendation. (d) `:201` `rrtmgp_role = "direct CKD compatibility baseline, not line-by-line truth"` — Pass #4423 caveat (a) locked into artifact metadata. (e) `:231` markdown trailing paragraph reiterates the same caveat in human-readable form: *"RRTMGP is reported as a compatibility comparison between two CKD models, not as the absolute-accuracy reference."* (f) `:171-177` `official_32g_baseline_passed()` cross-references `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_accuracy.json` — reuses existing gate-1 evidence instead of recomputing, clean composition. (g) `:200` `frozen_diagnostic = "16-g canonical model locked as diagnostic at normalized objective 7.006"` surfaces the 16-g policy in the new artifact's metadata. **Substantive concerns**: (1) **Acceptance rule at `:184-194` too permissive**: status `passed` requires only `ecckd_baseline_passed && all_finite_metrics`. There is **no quantitative bound on RRTMGP comparison metrics** — a 32-g model with `flux_rmse_vs_rrtmgp = 50 W/m²` would still pass. Recommend tightening to `flux_rmse_vs_rrtmgp < <bound>` per Pass #4423; suggested bound is ~1.0 W/m² (RRTMGP's own internal LBL accuracy spread is ~0.5 W/m², so a 32-g ecCKD model agreeing with RRTMGP within 1.0 W/m² is reasonable CKD-CKD compatibility). (2) **`fluxes_from_dataset` at `:65-72`** lacks `haskey(ds, name)` checks — string-concatenation NC variable name like `"radiative_heating_lw_up_clear"` would `KeyError` instead of producing a clear error message if the schema drifts. Minor. (3) **Hardcoded `gases.o2 = 0.20946` / `gases.n2 = 0.78084` at `:81-82`** — same hardcode as `rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl:56-57`. Correct for the three production-target columns (clear-sky/RCEMIP/tropical all use standard fractions), but no defensive read from `Array(ds["o2"])` / `Array(ds["n2"])` when those exist. Worth a comment. (4) **Hardcoded `surface_emissivity = 0.98` at `:100`** — the source NetCDF files do contain `lw_emissivity` arrays; could read from `Array(ds["lw_emissivity"])[:, column]` for band-resolved emissivity. Acceptable as broadband but flag. (5) **Markdown table at `:222-228` shows only flux RMSE and heating RMSE** — omits the `toa_forcing_abs_error` / `surface_forcing_abs_error` columns (which `aggregate_metrics` at `:133-134` does compute). The TOA/surface forcing errors are the strict gate-1 hard-threshold metrics; surfacing them in the markdown would make the 32-g production claim directly readable without diving into the JSON. **NOT yet landed** (Pass #4423 items still pending): (i) `radiative_heating.md` plan-level rewrite from "reduced 32→16" to "32-g production + RRTMGP comparison" — not done yet. (ii) `validation/goal_audit_check.jl:944-949` predicate replacement to consume this new artifact instead of the old 16-g `decision_required` requirement — not done yet. (iv) `validation/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl:1064 reduced_acceptance_decision_summary` update to declare the acceptance decision (`accepted_as_diagnostic` for 16-g) — not done yet. (v) `test/test_reduced_ecckd_32g_rrtmgp_comparison.jl` — no test file exists to lock the new artifact's invariants; recommend mirroring `test/test_rrtmgp_target_16g_ad_calibration.jl`'s structure with `@test result["status"] == "passed"`, `@test occursin("ecckd_clear_sky_tropical_column", json)`, `@test result["official_32g_ecckd_hard_gate_passed"]`, and at least one quantitative `@test result["cases"][1]["candidate_vs_rrtmgp"]["flux_rmse"] < 1.0` (or whatever bound is chosen). **Audit pipeline note**: the new artifact exists but the audit emitter doesn't yet ingest it, so `goal_audit_check.md:89` will still show `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance` for goal step 2 until item (ii) of the roadmap lands. Excellent first-step progress; the framing and infrastructure-reuse are exactly right. Canonical 16-g 7.006 unchanged (now permanently locked as diagnostic per `:200` artifact metadata).

Pass #4428 at 2026-05-19 03:27 UTC: no source changes since last poll.

Pass #4429 at 2026-05-19 03:29 UTC: **First result emitted** — `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_32g_rrtmgp_comparison.md` ran with three cases. **ecCKD reference comparisons all pass cleanly**: `:14` ecckd_clear_sky_tropical_column 0.00381 W/m² flux RMSE / 5.76e-8 K/s heating RMSE, `:15` ecckd_rcemip_style_column_subset 0.00309 / 4.41e-8, `:16` ecckd_all_sky_tropical_column_clear_projection 0.0586 / 6.94e-8 — all 4-orders-of-magnitude under the 0.30 W/m² gate-1 hard threshold. Confirms the 32-g ecCKD production target reproduces the ecRad reference solidly (already known from gate 1; now restated in this new artifact). **RRTMGP comparison values are CATASTROPHIC and physically implausible**: `:14` 152.0 W/m² flux RMSE, `:15` 108.9 W/m², `:16` 153.6 W/m². Two CKD models compared against each other on the same atmosphere should agree within O(1-5 W/m²) at worst — 100-150 W/m² is **two orders of magnitude beyond plausible CKD-CKD spread**. RRTMGP itself has ~0.5 W/m² LBL spread; ecCKD-rgb-32 has ~0.3 W/m² LBL spread; the worst-case CKD-CKD difference should be ~2-5 W/m². **150 W/m² indicates a bug, not real model divergence.** Possible root causes worth checking: (a) **TOA-incoming flux convention mismatch** — `validation/reduced_ecckd_32g_rrtmgp_comparison.jl:102` passes `toa_shortwave_down = Array(ds["solar_irradiance"])[column]` to `RRTMGPBoundaryConditions`, but if the NetCDF's `solar_irradiance` is already cos-zenith-projected while RRTMGP applies cos-zenith internally, the result would be double-projected. (b) **Spectral coverage mismatch** — ecCKD-rgb-32 has 5 visible-band split with specific boundaries; RRTMGP-SW has 14 bands with different spectral coverage (e.g., RRTMGP includes far-UV / near-IR extensions ecCKD-rgb-32 doesn't). The band-integrated total flux would differ by the missing/extra spectral range — could explain a ~50-200 W/m² systematic offset depending on how the package wrapper handles spectral boundaries. (c) **Surface albedo / emissivity not actually consumed by RRTMGP** — `:100` `surface_emissivity = 0.98` is hardcoded; the source NetCDF files have `lw_emissivity` arrays (Pass #4427 concern (4)). Hardcoded value may differ from what generated the reference fluxes. (d) **The ecRad reference fluxes are NOT what the package's 32-g ecCKD path produced**; per `:147-148`, `candidate = fluxes_from_dataset(ds, "radiative_heating_", spec.candidate_suffix, column)` reads `radiative_heating_lw_up`/etc. (the package's outputs) while `reference = fluxes_from_dataset(ds, "", spec.reference_suffix, column)` reads `lw_up`/etc. (the ecRad reference). The 0.003-0.06 W/m² ecCKD comparison values match because both are CKD models with the same support; the 150 W/m² RRTMGP comparison is a real cross-model gap that the package's wrapper may be amplifying. **Despite catastrophic RRTMGP numbers, status = `passed` at `:3`** — exactly the "too permissive acceptance rule" concern from Pass #4427. With a `flux_rmse_vs_rrtmgp < 1.0 W/m²` bound the status would correctly be `failed` and surface this issue. **The artifact IS doing its job** of computing and surfacing the RRTMGP cross-model gap; the issues are: (1) the acceptance rule doesn't gate on it, (2) the 150 W/m² value is implausibly large and indicates an upstream bug worth investigating before adopting this comparison as a production gate. **Heating-rate RMSE 5e-4 K/s ≈ 43 K/day** at `:14, 16` is also large (gate-1 heating-rate spread is ~0.35 K/day) but proportionally less than the flux gap — consistent with a global multiplicative flux scaling error rather than per-layer physics differences. **Recommended next steps for the implementing agent**: (a) **Debug the 150 W/m² RRTMGP flux gap before continuing the roadmap** — a 32-g model claimed to "match ecCKD" should also reasonably match RRTMGP within O(few W/m²) since both are calibrated against LBL on overlapping atmospheres. The actual root cause matters for downstream confidence in the comparison artifact. (b) Once debug resolves to plausible values, **tighten acceptance rule at `:184-194`** to include `flux_rmse_vs_rrtmgp < <bound>` (suggest 1.0-3.0 W/m² depending on observed clean-run values). (c) Pass #4423 items (i)-(ii)/(iv)/(v) still pending — but those depend on this artifact actually being meaningful, so debugging the RRTMGP gap should come first. **Bottom-line**: gate-1 still solidly passes, 32-g ecCKD production target confirmed, but the RRTMGP comparison axis needs root-cause analysis before it can be a real gate. Canonical 16-g 7.006 still locked as diagnostic per `:6`.

Pass #4430 at 2026-05-19 03:30 UTC: no source changes since last poll.

Pass #4431 at 2026-05-19 03:30 UTC: no source changes since last poll.

Pass #4432 at 2026-05-19 03:32 UTC: **MAJOR cascade — Pass #4423 roadmap items (ii), (iii), (v) all landed simultaneously**. (A) **NEW `test/test_reduced_ecckd_32g_rrtmgp_comparison.jl`** (42 lines) — Pass #4427 concern (6) addressed. Test invariants: `:11,22-23` lock `status: passed` in both markdown and JSON; `:24` locks `production_target == "official ecCKD 32-g gas optics"` (Pass #4423's exact framing); `:27` locks the *"not line-by-line truth"* caveat verbatim in `rrtmgp_role`; `:28` requires at least 3 cases; `:31-33` lock per-case names; **`:36` `flux_rmse < 0.30`** for ecCKD reference — excellent, locks gate-1 hard threshold compliance; **`:37` `heating_rate_rmse < 0.05`** — strict regression guard (current is 5e-8 K/s, so the bound is ~1e6× looser, but reasonable as a regression-catcher). **CRITICAL CONCERN at `:38-39`**: `@test isfinite(case["candidate_vs_rrtmgp"]["flux_rmse"])` — **only checks `isfinite`, not a quantitative bound**. The 152.0 W/m² RRTMGP flux RMSE from Pass #4429 would still satisfy this test. The Pass #4427/#4429 "too permissive acceptance rule" concern has now been **propagated from the artifact to the test**, locking the physically implausible RRTMGP gap as the contractual state. Anyone debugging the 150 W/m² issue would get no signal from the test that "fixed" means anything specific. (B) **Audit md cascade at `validation/results/goal_audit_check.md`** — comprehensive reframing: `:90` goal step 2 **renamed** from `reduced ecCKD/RRTMG-compatible gas optics` → **`32-g ecCKD/RRTMGP-compatible gas optics`** AND **status flipped** from `decision_required_on_reduced_acceptance` → **`passed_32g_target`**. Description text now: *"Use the validated official ecCKD 32-g production model, freeze the 16-g model as a diagnostic, and emit direct RRTMGP comparison metrics on the same column ensemble."* — exactly Pass #4423's intent. `:103` checklist row: `32-g ecCKD/RRTMGP-compatible production gas optics | satisfied` with evidence list including the new `32-g ecCKD/RRTMGP comparison (verified)` and **reframed 16-g entries**: `reduced 16-g diagnostic hard-threshold record (verified)` and `reduced 16-g diagnostic optimization objective record (verified)` — the old 16-g blockers have been gracefully relabeled as diagnostic records rather than dropped. (C) **NEW audit entries** at `:31-32, 63`: `package-native RRTMGP comparison extension | verified | false`, `package-native RRTMGP comparison test | verified | false`, `32-g ecCKD/RRTMGP comparison | verified | false`. **Roadmap completion status**: ✅ item (iii) artifact (Pass #4427), ✅ item (v) test (this pass), ✅ item (ii) audit-emitter predicate replacement (this pass evidenced by `:90` and `:103` reframing). Pending: ❓ item (i) `radiative_heating.md` plan-level rewrite — would need separate verification. ❓ item (iv) `validation/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.jl:1064` `reduced_acceptance_decision_summary` update — likely still recommending the old 16-g options. The closing-line evolution at `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_gap_report.md:1735` (the 15-refinement paragraph) probably still says the 16-g decision is `decision_required` — that's a documentation gap. **CRITICAL CONCERN: Goal step 2 audit now claims `passed_32g_target` despite Pass #4429's 150 W/m² RRTMGP flux gap**. The reframing is logically correct (we did move to 32-g and we did emit the RRTMGP comparison), but the substantive bug (RRTMGP-vs-ecCKD-32g flux disagreement of 100-150 W/m² is two orders of magnitude beyond plausible CKD-CKD spread) is now papered over by a `passed` status at the audit, artifact, AND test layers. Three concentric reinforcing locks make this bug very hard to surface unless someone reads the actual numerical values. **Recommended next-step for the implementing agent** (now urgent): (1) **Debug the 150 W/m² RRTMGP flux gap** — likely candidate: TOA-incoming-flux double-projection (the package's RRTMGPClearSkyModel may apply `cos_zenith` internally while the test passes already-projected `solar_irradiance`); OR spectral coverage mismatch between ecCKD-rgb-32 and RRTMGP-SW band sets; OR the package's `RRTMGPClearSkyModel` returns un-normalized band-internal fluxes. (2) **Once values are debugged** to plausible O(few W/m²), **tighten `test/test_reduced_ecckd_32g_rrtmgp_comparison.jl:38-39` from `@test isfinite(...)` to `@test ... < <bound>`** (recommend 2.0-5.0 W/m² for RRTMGP-vs-ecCKD-32g; tightest defensible bound is the union of each model's individual LBL spread, ~1.0 W/m²). (3) **Update `validation/reduced_ecckd_32g_rrtmgp_comparison.jl:184-194`** acceptance rule in lockstep with the test (single source of truth for the bound). **Bottom-line on gate-2 narrative**: cosmetic resolution achieved — audit claims `passed_32g_target` — but the underlying RRTMGP comparison axis is currently broken-but-locked-as-passing. The framing is right; the numbers are wrong. Canonical 16-g 7.006 remains permanently frozen as diagnostic per `:103`.

- Pass #5343 at 2026-05-19T18:54:28Z: NEW FILE `Artifacts.toml` at repo root (first source-tree change in ~14 hours). Single artifact `ecrad_data` (lines 1-7), `git-tree-sha1 = "49ce668ce0861f9d5e8299d68af7138485eb5f19"`, lazy, pointing at `ecmwf-ifs/ecrad` commit `131ac980` as tarball with SHA256 pin. **Issues:** (a) **No consumer.** `grep "artifact\"ecrad_data\"\|LazyArtifacts"` across `src/AnalyticBandRadiation.jl` and `Project.toml` returns zero hits — the artifact is declared but unwired, so it will not be downloaded by tests or scripts. Plan-level expectation (`radiative_heating.md` mentions `ecrad_data` 3× as a CI-time pull) is not yet satisfied; needs a paired edit to `Project.toml` adding `LazyArtifacts` UUID `4af54fe1-eca0-43a8-85a7-787d91b784e3` to `[deps]` and a `using LazyArtifacts` + `artifact"ecrad_data"` reference in whatever validation script reads ecRad NetCDFs. (b) **URL points at full repo tarball, not the data subdirectory.** `https://github.com/ecmwf-ifs/ecrad/archive/131ac980....tar.gz` is the entire ecrad source tree (Fortran, Makefiles, docs, etc.), not just the reference NetCDFs we need. Wastes ~tens of MB per cache and pins the artifact to the *code* commit rather than the *data* commit. Prefer a release tarball if available or a separately archived data-only tarball. (c) **Coexistence with `validation/external/ecrad/` not addressed.** The in-tree ecRad checkout is still untracked and present; if the artifact replaces it for CI consumers the .gitignore + plan need to say so explicitly, otherwise downstream users will have two divergent ecRad copies (in-tree vs. artifact cache). (d) **`git-tree-sha1` reproducibility.** The `git-tree-sha1` is the hash of the *unpacked artifact tree*, not the upstream commit — that's fine and correct for Pkg artifact format. But the upstream commit `131ac980` is not a tagged release; verify it's on `master` or a long-lived branch, otherwise an upstream force-push invalidates the URL while the SHA256 keeps verifying. (e) **No `os/arch` block.** Correct for a data-only artifact, no action needed; flagging only to confirm it was deliberate.

- Pass #5346 at 2026-05-19T18:57:52Z: `src/io/ecckd_definition.jl` updated (411 lines) to consume the Pass #5343 `Artifacts.toml`. Verified `Project.toml:10` adds `LazyArtifacts` UUID `4af54fe1-eca0-43a8-85a7-787d91b784e3` to [deps] with compat `Project.toml:29` = `"1"`, and `src/AnalyticBandRadiation.jl:6` has `using LazyArtifacts`. Pass #5343 concern (a) "no consumer" is **resolved**. New observations: (a) `_ecrad_artifact_root` at `src/io/ecckd_definition.jl:92-101` wraps `@artifact_str("ecrad_data")` in a bare `try/catch ... return nothing`. This swallows checksum mismatches, network failures, and missing-artifact errors identically, then silently falls through to the in-tree `validation/external/ecrad/` checkout. A user with a corrupted artifact cache will never see the error. **Suggest:** `@warn` the caught exception before returning `nothing`, or catch only `ArgumentError`/`InitError`-class artifact errors. (b) `_ecrad_data_file` at `:122-135` walks `readdir(root)` unfiltered to find a child with a `data/` subdir. Github tarballs extract to a single `ecrad-<sha>/` directory so this works today, but the loop materializes a full `readdir` per call — cache the resolved data dir behind a `Ref` or compute it once at module init. Also, the comment in the docstring at `:103-109` does not document the `<child>/data/` fallback layer, only the three top-level candidates. (c) `_OFFICIAL_ECCKD_DEFAULTS` at `:69-75` mixes ecCKD versions 1.0 / 1.2 / 1.4 — confirm the pinned ecRad commit `131ac980` actually ships all six filenames in `_OFFICIAL_ECCKD_MODELS` at `:60-67`; otherwise `official_ecckd_definition_path(:shortwave_96)` will throw at runtime on a fresh artifact pull. (d) `read_ecckd_definition(path::AbstractString)` at `:276-278` throws `ArgumentError` without naming the extension to load. Line `:290` (the tabulated-gas-optics variant) names NCDatasets explicitly; mirror that text at `:277` for consistency: `"... load NCDatasets.jl before calling it"`. (e) Pass #5343 critique (b) — that the artifact URL grabs the full ecRad source tarball when only `data/` is needed — remains unaddressed and is now embedded in the resolution code: the `<child>/data/` walk at `:127-129` is the *only* reason this code works against a full-repo tarball. A data-only tarball would let `:125` (`root/data/<file>`) match directly and `:127-129` go away. (f) Default runtime pair at `:170-177` is `(longwave=:longwave_64, shortwave=:shortwave_32)` — confirm against `radiative_heating.md`: the plan's "reduced 32x16" gate refers to 32-g LW + 16-g SW, not the official 64x32; this defaults file is for the *official* reference pair only, which is correct, but the reduced 16-g SW is a separate custom artifact and worth a docstring note here clarifying that `official_ecckd_definition_paths` returns only the upstream-shipped pair.
- Pass #5347 at 2026-05-19T18:58:52Z: NEW `test/test_ecckd_artifacts.jl` (21 lines) — paired coverage for the Pass #5346 artifact wiring. Issues: (a) **Silent skip at `:15-18`.** If neither the artifact nor the in-tree `validation/external/ecrad/` checkout is present, the test logs an `@info` and falls through to `@test true` — vacuous pass. CI without the data installed will green-light this test while never exercising the artifact code path. Use `@test_skip` (or fail-by-default with a documented opt-out env var) instead of `@test true`. (b) **Env-var resolution at `src/io/ecckd_definition.jl:111` is untested.** `ecrad_data_path` walks `RH_ECRAD_DATA_PATH` first; this branch has no coverage in the new test. (c) **Artifact code path not distinguished from in-tree fallback.** `require=false` everywhere and both branches return a non-`nothing` path that pass `isdir`/`isfile`. The test cannot detect a regression in `_ecrad_artifact_root` because the in-tree checkout silently covers for it. To verify the Artifacts.toml wiring actually works, mask the in-tree checkout (e.g. via `RH_ECRAD_DATA_PATH=""` semantics + a temp `validation/external/ecrad/` rename trick or a custom `ecrad_data_path` override) and assert the artifact root is returned. (d) **Pass #5346 (a) silent `try/catch` not exercised.** No test injects a corrupted/missing artifact to confirm the error is swallowed as designed (or to catch a future regression that converts the catch into an unintended throw). (e) **Default-naming contract at `:13-14`** is brittle: `basename(paths.longwave) == "ecckd-1.2_lw_climate_narrow-64b_ckd-definition.nc"` pins the default `(longwave=:longwave_64, shortwave=:shortwave_32)` argument mapping. A defensible contract, but worth a comment saying "this test exists to lock the default; change the default and this test intentionally". (f) **No coverage of the `<child>/data/` walk in `_ecrad_data_file:127-129`** — the github-tarball-shape fallback path is the load-bearing one for the current Artifacts.toml URL, yet only the direct `root/data/<file>` shape is exercised when the in-tree checkout is present. Synthetic-fixture test recommended.
- Pass #5348 at 2026-05-19T19:00:50Z: three changes — NEW `validation/ecckd_model_inventory.jl` (123 lines), NEW `test/test_ecckd_model_inventory.jl` (24 lines), MODIFIED `test/runtests.jl` (added `include("test_ecckd_artifacts.jl")` at `:18` and `include("test_ecckd_model_inventory.jl")` at `:19`). Observations: **validation script:** (a) `validation/ecckd_model_inventory.jl:5` falls back to `push!(LOAD_PATH, ABR_ROOT)` — defensive but anti-pattern; `test_ecckd_model_inventory.jl:4` already passes `--project=$root` so this path is unreachable from the test harness. Either remove or comment as standalone-only. (b) `:86` conflates "all schemas valid" with "all files present" — `official_ecckd_definition_path(filename)` at `:56` defaults to `require=true`, so a missing file throws before `entry.valid_schema` is ever set. A partial-failure report path (catch per-file, set `entry.error = ...`, status `"partial"` if any missing) would surface per-file failures rather than aborting at the first gap. (c) `:87` and `:56` redundantly assert `require=true` on `ecrad_data_path`; second call is no-op once any file has been resolved. **test:** (d) `test/test_ecckd_model_inventory.jl:4` shells out a fresh Julia subprocess — same cold-start cost antipattern flagged in Pass #7 for `test_goal_audit_check.jl`. Repeats it instead of refactoring: export `run_ecckd_model_inventory()` from the validation script and call it in-process. (e) `:6-9, 17-23` use `occursin` against raw stdout AND JSON byte-substrings — quadruple brittleness (subprocess + stdout text-match + writer-order-dependent JSON substring + global-substring-anywhere). (f) **Genuine semantic bug at `:22-23`:** `@test occursin("\\"source_tables_present\\": true", json)` and `...\\"rayleigh_tables_present\\": true...` pass if the literal substring appears *anywhere* in the JSON. Per `src/io/ecckd_definition.jl:319`, `source_tables_present` is computed from LW-source-variable presence (Planck function, lw_source, etc.); pure-SW ecCKD files such as `ecckd-1.4_sw_climate_rgb-32b` and `ecckd-1.4_sw_climate_vfine-96b` should report `source_tables_present: false`. The test currently passes as long as *any one* LW file in the inventory reports `true`, so it cannot catch a regression where the predicate accidentally fires `true` for all files (or `false` for all). Tighten: assert the exact per-entry array, e.g. `[true, false, true, false, false, false]` matching the LW/SW classification of the 6 official files. (g) `:19-21` asserts gpoints `32`, `64`, `96` appear somewhere — same any-match weakness; pin the exact multiset `{32×3, 64×2, 96×1}` matching `_OFFICIAL_ECCKD_MODELS` at `src/io/ecckd_definition.jl:60-67`. **runtests:** (h) insertion order `:18-19` is reasonable (definition → artifacts → inventory → ncdatasets ext), but the subprocess-shelled inventory test runs before the in-process `test_ecckd_ncdatasets_ext.jl`, so if NCDatasets fails to load in the test subprocess it will surface here first with a cryptic shell error rather than a clean ext-loading-failure diagnostic. Move `test_ecckd_ncdatasets_ext.jl` ahead of the subprocess-shelled test.
Pass #5349 at 2026-05-19T19:01:14Z: no source changes since last poll.
Pass #5350 at 2026-05-19T19:01:30Z: no source changes since last poll.
- Pass #5351 at 2026-05-19T19:03:47Z: three more changes — NEW `validation/ecckd_recovery_metrics.jl` (231 lines), NEW `test/test_ecckd_recovery_metrics.jl` (25 lines), MODIFIED `test/runtests.jl:20` adding the new include. **Substantive concerns:** (a) **Default mode is self-recovery only.** `validation/ecckd_recovery_metrics.jl:171-184` defaults `reference = candidate = official_ecckd_definition_path(:shortwave_32)` and labels the run `recovery_mode = "published_self_recovery_sanity"` (`:180`). The test only exercises this trivial degenerate case — `test_ecckd_recovery_metrics.jl:21-23` asserts exact zero error, which only holds when reading the same file twice. There is no test (yet) for the actual gate-2 use case — comparing a reduced 16-g candidate against the published reference — which is what this tool is presumably *for*. (b) **Threshold appropriateness suspect for the real use case.** Thresholds at `:15-19` (`COEFFICIENT_LOG_RMSE_THRESHOLD = 1e-3`, `MEDIAN_RELATIVE_ERROR_THRESHOLD = 1e-3`, `P99_RELATIVE_ERROR_THRESHOLD = 1e-2`, `GPOINT_WEIGHT_ABS_ERROR_THRESHOLD = 1e-6`, `BAND_WEIGHT_ABS_ERROR_THRESHOLD = 1e-8`) are tight for self-recovery (which is exact) but likely overly tight for candidate-vs-published — the plan's reduced 16-g model at normalized objective 7.006 against a 1.0 target will almost certainly produce per-coefficient log-RMSE well above `1e-3`. Status will be `"failed"` when wiring this to gate 2. Worth pre-discussing realistic thresholds. (c) **API is single-pair.** `run_ecckd_recovery_metrics(reference, candidate)` produces one report. Gate 2 needs paired LW+SW reports; consumers will need to run the script twice and merge or refactor to accept a pair. (d) **Coefficient-only comparison.** `comparable_coefficient_names` at `:59-68` only matches `*_molar_absorption_coeff` variables — Planck function, solar irradiance, Rayleigh tables, and gpoint→band mapping are not directly compared. Aggregated g-point + band weight checks (`:128-136`) partially catch mapping/weighting drift, but a candidate could pass coefficient comparison while having broken Planck/solar tables. (e) **Test pins the threshold, not a measured value.** `test/test_ecckd_recovery_metrics.jl:24` asserts `occursin("\\"log_coefficient_rmse\\": 0.001", json)` which matches the threshold echoed in the JSON. Tightening `COEFFICIENT_LOG_RMSE_THRESHOLD` requires a lock-step edit to the test — same brittleness as Pass #7. (f) **Same subprocess-shell antipattern** (`test_ecckd_recovery_metrics.jl:5`) as Pass #5348 (d) and Pass #7. Three uses of this pattern now (`test_goal_audit_check`, `test_ecckd_model_inventory`, `test_ecckd_recovery_metrics`) — worth promoting a shared "run-validation-and-assert" helper that calls in-process. (g) Minor — `gpoint_fraction` fallback at `:99` assumes this variable exists in published ecCKD files; verify against the pinned commit's actual variable names. (h) `test/runtests.jl:20` ordering still places the subprocess test before the ext-loading smoke test (same flag as Pass #5348 h).
Pass #5352 at 2026-05-19T19:04:05Z: no source changes since last poll.
- Pass #5353 at 2026-05-19T19:05:22Z: NEW `validation/results/official_ecckd_definition_files_check.md` (33 lines, generated artifact). **Confirms Pass #5348 (f) critique was correct**: `:9` shows LW `source_tables_present=true, rayleigh=false`, `:10` shows SW `source_tables_present=false, rayleigh=true` — the SW model legitimately reports `source_tables_present: false`. The Pass #5348-flagged `test_ecckd_model_inventory.jl:22` assertion `occursin("\\"source_tables_present\\": true", json)` passes only because the LW entry has `true` somewhere in the JSON; it cannot distinguish correct from incorrect per-file classification. Tighten that assertion. **Bigger concern surfaced by `:18-23`:** the runtime LUT ingestion ships with only `h2o, co2` gases (`Gas names: `h2o, co2``, line :18) and absorption tables of shape `[64, 2, 53, 6]` / `[32, 2, 53, 6]` (`:22, :23`) — the second axis size 2 is the gas count. The plan's gates require O3 + N2O + CH4 + O2 alongside H2O+CO2 (ecRad's `ColumnAtmosphere` populates h2o, o3, co2, ch4, n2o, o2, n2 per `validation/reduced_ecckd_32g_rrtmgp_comparison.jl:79-87`). With only H2O+CO2 loaded, any column with non-trivial O3 (stratosphere) or CH4/N2O will systematically under-absorb. The check still emits `Status: **passed**` (`:3`, `:14`) because schema validation passes for the 2 gases that are loaded — but the missing-gas case is not flagged. Sanity-check before downstream consumers depend on this LUT. **Other:** `:20` shows pressure grid size 53, `:21` temperature grid `[53, 6]` — confirms ecCKD canonical `(pressure, ratio)` × 6 reference T per pressure layout. `:28` Planck source table `[64, 231]` matches the ecCKD published Planck-function dimension count (231 sample temperatures from 100 K to 350 K in 1.087 K steps roughly). `:26` Rayleigh max optical-depth 1.0099 across 32 SW g-points indicates the shortest-wavelength bin reaches τ≈1 — plausible.
Pass #5354 at 2026-05-19T19:05:48Z: no source changes since last poll.
- Pass #5355 at 2026-05-19T19:07:40Z: NEW `validation/ecckd_teacher_student_recovery.jl` (187 lines) — Enzyme-reverse-mode + Reactant-compile-check smoke gate. **Substantive concerns:** (a) **Step size at `:88` is exactly the Newton step for the quadratic loss.** `step = length(parameters) / 2` and `L = sum((p-t)²)/N` gives `p ← p - (N/2)·(2(p-t)/N) = p - (p-t) = t` in **one** iteration. The 8-iteration loop at `:89-94` converges exactly on iteration 1 and the other 7 iterations are zero-gradient no-ops (wasted Enzyme AD calls). The "loss reduction factor" at `:133` divides `first ≈ 1e-3` by `last ≈ eps` → astronomical ratio (`~4.5e15`) reported as evidence of "recovery". (b) **Initial perturbation is unrealistically small at `:40`** (`0.02 sin + 0.01 cos`, |Δ| ≤ 0.03 in log space → ~3% relative coefficient perturbation). The actual reduced-16-g candidate is at normalized objective 7.006 vs 1.0 target — orders of magnitude worse than this test exercises. So the "recovery" here certifies nothing about real model-reduction quality. (c) **Naming is misleading.** This is not a "teacher-student recovery"; it is a "Julia AD-and-compile stack smoke check": ⟨can Enzyme compute a gradient?, can Reactant compile a kernel on CPU?⟩. The published ecCKD coefficients are *both* teacher and student here. Recommend renaming the script and `case` field (`:122`) to `ecckd_ad_compile_smoke` or similar. (d) **Hidden Reactant requirement at `:62-82`.** `Base.find_package("Reactant") === nothing` returns `(status="not_available", passed=false)`, and `:120` requires `reactant_check.passed` for `status="passed"`. So this script silently *demands* Reactant be installed, despite using `Base.PkgId` to avoid a hard Project.toml dep. CI without Reactant → `status="failed"` reported with status string `"not_available"` in the inner check. Either make Reactant a hard test dep or downgrade the requirement to "available implies passed; absent implies skip". (e) **`first_error_line(err)` at `:80` is undefined** in this file and in the included `ecckd_recovery_metrics.jl` — `grep "function first_error_line\|first_error_line ="` returns nothing on those two files. The `catch` branch will throw `UndefVarError` instead of returning a structured failure. Use `sprint(showerror, err)` or define the helper. (f) **Reactant CPU-only at `:69`** doesn't validate the eventual H100 gate-3 codegen. Reasonable for a smoke check but should be documented as a CPU-only smoke (the production gate-3 requires H100 multi-gas compile). (g) **In-place NetCDF mutation at `:99-104`**: `cp` then open-with-mode-`"a"` and overwrite specific variables. Safer to materialize a new file from scratch with only the necessary variables + preserved attributes. (h) Hard-coded 8 iterations at `gradient_descent_recover(initial, target; iterations=8)` (`:84`) — with the Newton-step bug at (a), even 1 iteration suffices; with realistic perturbation magnitudes (item b), 8 may not. Either replace the constant step with a line search, or document the converges-in-1 property and drop iterations to 1.

(b) `:25` is a duplicate of `:22` — both `occursin("\\"status\\": \\"passed\\"", json)`, and the comment-less intent (the second one is presumably meant to check the nested `recovery_metrics.status`) is invisible to `occursin`; the test passes if *any* status field anywhere in the JSON is `"passed"`. Good catch. **Issues:** (a) **Pass #5355 (a) Newton-step bug NOT fixed.** Step `length(parameters) / 2` at `:90` unchanged. Scan mode now amplifies the waste — 6 files × 8 iterations × ~173K-parameter Enzyme reverse-mode gradients per file = ~8M needlessly-recomputed gradients across the scan. (b) **Pass #5355 (e) `first_error_line(err)` at `:82` still undefined.** Any caught Reactant compile error will throw `UndefVarError` instead of returning a clean failure record. Critically, the scan-mode `all(... .status == "passed", results)` at `:160` won't even reach the failure path — the `UndefVarError` propagates up through `run_ecckd_teacher_student_recovery_for_filename` and aborts the scan rather than producing "5 passed, 1 failed". (c) **Scan writes 6 recovered candidate NetCDFs** to `validation/results/`, each ~10-100 MB copy of an official file. Total ~hundreds of MB of repo-resident generated files. Needs `.gitignore` for `validation/results/*_recovered_candidate.nc` or cleanup. (d) **`safe_candidate_filename` at `:100-101`** does `replace(..., "_ckd-definition.nc" => "_recovered_candidate.nc")` then `replace(..., "-" => "_")`. The second replace turns `ecckd-1.4_sw_climate_rgb-32b` into `ecckd_1.4_sw_climate_rgb_32b`. Lossy and collision-risky for files differing only in dash/underscore position; not a real risk for the 6 official files but unjustified. (e) **6 cold Reactant compiles per scan** at `:78` — each ~10s+. Scan runtime will be dominated by Reactant XLA compile overhead. If scan is opt-in via `RH_ECCKD_TEACHER_STUDENT_SCAN=true` (`:242`) and not in CI, this is acceptable but worth documenting. (f) **No paired test for scan mode.** `test/test_ecckd_teacher_student_recovery.jl` (Pass #5358) only exercises single-file mode; the `scan_main` entry point is unreachable from any `@testset`. (g) Env-var name `RH_ECCKD_TEACHER_STUDENT_SCAN` at `:242` consistent with `RH_ECRAD_DATA_PATH` from Pass #5346 — good. (h) **Scan still confines to "teacher == student"** semantics: every entry uses `reference = official_ecckd_definition_path(filename); candidate = recovered-from-perturbed-target`. Pass #5355 (c) "this is an AD/compile smoke, not real model reduction" critique applies to every row of the scan as well — 6 trivial recoveries instead of 1.

NEW results artifact `validation/results/ecckd_teacher_student_recovery_scan.md` shows scan ran successfully — all 6 files `status=passed`, Final loss = 0.0, Worst log RMSE = 0.0, identical p99 relative error `2.4424906541753444e-15` (machine epsilon). **Empirical confirmation of Pass #5361 (a)**: every row exhibits the Newton-step convergence-in-one-iteration signature; ~8M Enzyme gradient calls across the scan produced zero learning beyond iteration 1. **Confirms file-format observation**: rows 8 + 11 both report exactly 172,992 parameters for `ecckd-1.0_sw_climate_rgb-32b` and `ecckd-1.4_sw_climate_rgb-32b` — suggesting the SW32 rgb file is unchanged between ecCKD 1.0 and 1.4, which defends the version-mixing in `_OFFICIAL_ECCKD_DEFAULTS` flagged in Pass #5346 (c). **Test design concerns:** (a) **Cached-artifact short-circuit at `:6-12`** runs the scan only if results files don't already exist. Should rerun unconditionally OR compare artifact-mtime ≥ script-mtime. (b) **Hardcoded `"\"parameter_count\": 518976"` at `:28`** pins to a specific ecRad commit's file shape. Updating `Artifacts.toml` invalidates this test without any signal that the artifact change is the cause. (c) **`count(==('{'), json) >= 7` at `:27`** is a fragile structural check — works today because JSON has 1 top-level + 6 entry objects, but `reactant_compile_check` and `recovery_metrics` are also nested objects, so the actual count is much higher; the `>=7` bound is weaker than it could be. (d) **No per-file pass assertion** — relies on top-level `status: passed` aggregated by `validation/ecckd_teacher_student_recovery.jl:160` via `all(... .status == "passed", results)`. If that aggregation regresses to `any`, the test still passes. (e) **8-column table contract not pinned** — only 3 filenames asserted to appear (`:19-21`); the table's columns (Status, Parameters, Initial loss, Final loss, Reactant, Worst log RMSE, Worst P99 relative error) are not asserted, so a column drop would silently pass the test. (f) **Pass #5361 (a) Newton-step bug still NOT fixed**; empirically wasted ~8M Enzyme gradients across this scan. (g) **Pass #5361 (b) `first_error_line` still undefined** — every Reactant compile happened to pass this run, but a future failure throws UndefVarError. (h) **Pass #5361 (c) recovered NetCDFs** now physically present in `validation/results/` (6 files, hundreds of MB total) — still need `.gitignore`.

Any upstream rewording, column reorder, or whitespace change breaks the parser silently. **Strong recommend:** read the JSON siblings instead. (b) Fragile field-count guards at `:57` (`== 6`) and `:85` (`== 7`) — adding a column to either upstream emits silently-dropped rows → `status="missing_accuracy_rows"` (`:202`). Noisy failure but fragile-by-design. (c) **Pareto metric collapses LW+SW** at `:104` (sorts on `total_gpoints`, breaks ties by `worst_boundary_forcing_error_w_m2`). A 32+16 point and a 16+32 point and a 24+24 point all collide at `total_gpoints=48`, and the plot can't distinguish them — but the gate-2 design point is specifically (32 LW, 16 SW), not "any 48-g LW+SW combination". Worth at least adding an annotation column. (d) **`max(toa, surface)` at `:73`** flattens two independent 0.30 W/m² constraints into one — the threshold line at `:174-176` is drawn against this max, not each independently. A column passing TOA at 0.1 W/m² but failing surface at 0.5 W/m² ends up at 0.5 W/m² in the plot, fine, but a column passing TOA at 0.4 W/m² and surface at 0.2 W/m² shows 0.4 W/m² — equally a violation. The single-axis plot can't show the 2D constraint geometry. (e) Inconsistent error-handling: `tryparse` at `:59-61`, plain `parse` at `:88`, will throw on bad inventory row. (f) **Status `"passed"`** at `:202` essentially always succeeds if the upstream MD has any rows — this is a visualization tool, not a gate. Misleading status label. (g) **Honest disclaimer at `:211`**: notes explicitly say *"radiative accuracy for newly trained intermediate models remains future work"* — Pareto plots only existing rows, not the actual gate-2 candidate frontier. Good honesty. (h) **No paired test** — `grep test_ecckd_band_accuracy_pareto` returns nothing; script is not in `runtests.jl`. Visualization-only, no regression guard on parse logic. (i) SVG hand-built at `:135-192` — reasonable for one-off, but `:178` adds modulo-9 jitter on x; deterministic (good) but obscures the categorical x-axis meaning. (j) **Dependency on `validation/results/reduced_ecckd_accuracy.md`** which is not in current git status — verify present before script is run; otherwise Pareto report is empty.
